{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_7bc977d9e6f342148494989c","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Outcome routing: map agent exit codes to explicit handlers.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Every possible exit code has a named handler. The type system enforces\n4\t//! exhaustiveness — if an outcome can happen, it must have a handler.\n5\t//!\n6\t//! Depends on: `types`, `config`, `bead_store`, `telemetry`, `validation`.\n7\t\n8\tuse std::fmt;\n9\tuse std::sync::atomic::{AtomicBool, Ordering};\n10\tuse std::sync::Arc;\n11\t\n12\tuse anyhow::Result;\n13\tuse chrono::Utc;\n14\t\n15\tuse crate::bead_store::{BeadStore, SyncRecoveryError};\n16\tuse crate::config::Config;\n17\tuse crate::telemetry::{EventKind, Telemetry};\n18\t#[cfg(test)]\n19\tuse crate::types::BeadStatus;\n20\tuse crate::types::{AgentOutcome, Bead, BeadAction, HandlerResult, Outcome};\n21\tuse crate::validation::{GateConfig, ValidationGate};\n22\t\n23\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n24\t// classify (convenience re-export)\n25\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n26\t\n27\t/// Classify an agent exit code into an `Outcome`, with shutdown signal support.\n28\t///\n29\t/// Delegates to `Outcome::classify()`. Provided here for ergonomic imports.\n30\tpub fn classify(exit_code: i32, was_interrupted: bool) -> Outcome {\n31\t    Outcome::classify(exit_code, was_interrupted)\n32\t}\n33\t\n34\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n35\t// OutcomeHandler\n36\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n37\t\n38\t/// Routes agent outcomes to their explicit handlers.\n39\tpub struct OutcomeHandler {\n40\t    config: Config,\n41\t    telemetry: Telemetry,\n42\t}\n43\t\n44\timpl OutcomeHandler {\n45\t    pub fn new(config: Config, telemetry: Telemetry) -> Self {\n46\t        OutcomeHandler { config, telemetry }\n47\t    }\n48\t\n49\t    /// Run a bead store operation with a 30s timeout.\n50\t    ///\n51\t    /// Returns `Ok(Some(T))` on success, `Ok(None)` on timeout, and `Err(E)` on\n52\t    /// other errors. Callers should treat timeout and error as non-fatal — log\n53\t    /// and continue rather than blocking the worker in HANDLING state.\n54\t    async fn timeout_op<T, F, Fut>(\n55\t        &self,\n56\t        op: F,\n57\t        operation_name: &str,\n58\t    ) -> Result<Option<T>, anyhow::Error>\n59\t    where\n60\t        F: FnOnce() -> Fut,\n61\t        Fut: std::future::Future<Output = Result<T, anyhow::Error>>,\n62\t    {\n63\t        match tokio::time::timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(30), op()).await {\n64\t            Ok(Ok(result)) => Ok(Some(result)),\n65\t            Ok(Err(e)) => Err(e),\n66\t            Err(_) => {\n67\t                tracing::error!(\n68\t                    operation = operation_name,\n69\t                    \"bead store operation timed out after 30s\"\n70\t                );\n71\t                Ok(None)\n72\t            }\n73\t        }\n74\t    }\n75\t\n76\t    /// Release a bead with sync conflict recovery and graceful failure handling.\n77\t    ///\n78\t    /// Flow:\n79\t    /// 1. Run `br sync --flush-only` to push local writes to JSONL first.\n80\t    /// 2. Attempt `br update` to release the bead (set status=open, assignee=\"\").\n81\t    /// 3. If SYNC_CONFLICT recovery fails, emit `BeadReleaseFailed` and return Ok.\n82\t    ///\n83\t    /// This method never returns an error — release failures are non-fatal.\n84\t    /// The worker can continue processing other beads even if release fails.\n85\t    async fn release_bead(&self, store: &dyn BeadStore, bead: &Bead) -> Result<Vec<EventKind>> {\n86\t        let mut events = Vec::new();\n87\t\n88\t        // Step 1: Flush local writes to JSONL before attempting release.\n89\t        // Emit heartbeat before br call to track where hang occurs.\n90\t        let _ = self.telemetry.emit_try_lock(EventKind::HeartbeatEmitted {\n91\t            bead_id: Some(bead.id.clone()),\n92\t            state: \"HANDLING_FLUSH\".to_string(),\n93\t        });\n94\t\n95\t        match self.timeout_op(|| store.flush(), \"flush\").await {\n96\t            Ok(Some(())) => {\n97\t                tracing::debug!(\n98\t                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n99\t                    \"flushed local changes to JSONL before release\"\n100\t                );\n101\t                // Emit heartbeat after successful flush.\n102\t                let _ = self.telemetry.emit_try_lock(EventKind::HeartbeatEmitted {\n103\t                    bead_id: Some(bead.id.clone()),\n104\t                    state: \"HANDLING_FLUSH_DONE\".to_string(),\n105\t                });\n106\t            }\n107\t            Ok(None) => {\n108\t                tracing::warn!(\n109\t                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n110\t                    \"flush timed out before release, attempting release anyway\"\n111\t                );\n112\t                events.push(EventKind::WorkerHandlingTimeout {\n113\t                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n114\t                    outcome: \"release\".to_string(),\n115\t                    operation: \"flush\".to_string(),\n116\t                    error: \"timeout after 30s\".to_string(),\n117\t                });\n118\t            }\n119\t            Err(e) => {\n120\t                tracing::warn!(\n121\t                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n122\t                    error = %e,\n123\t                    \"flush failed before release, attempting release anyway\"\n124\t                );\n125\t                events.push(EventKind::WorkerHandlingTimeout {\n126\t                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n127\t                    outcome: \"release\".to_string(),\n128\t                    operation: \"flush\".to_string(),\n129\t                    error: e.to_string(),\n130\t                });\n131\t            }\n132\t        }\n133\t\n134\t        // Step 2: Attempt release with timeout protection.\n135\t        // Emit heartbeat before br release call to track where hang occurs.\n136\t        let _ = self.telemetry.emit_try_lock(EventKind::HeartbeatEmitted {\n137\t            bead_id: Some(bead.id.clone()),\n138\t            state: \"HANDLING_RELEASE\".to_string(),\n139\t        });\n140\t\n141\t        let release_result =\n142\t            tokio::time::timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(30), store.release(&bead.id)).await;\n143\t\n144\t        match release_result {\n145\t            Ok(Ok(())) => {\n146\t                tracing::debug!(\n147\t                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n148\t                    \"bead released successfully\"\n149\t                );\n150\t                // Emit heartbeat after successful release.\n151\t                let _ = self.telemetry.emit_try_lock(EventKind::HeartbeatEmitted {\n152\t                    bead_id: Some(bead.id.clone()),\n153\t                    state: \"HANDLING_RELEASE_DONE\".to_string(),\n154\t                });\n155\t                events.push(EventKind::BeadReleased {\n156\t                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n157\t                    reason: \"release_success\".to_string(),\n158\t                });\n159\t            }\n160\t            Ok(Err(e)) => {\n161\t                // Release failed (br error, not timeout).\n162\t                // Check if this is a SyncRecoveryError (sync recovery was attempted but failed).\n163\t                if e.downcast_ref::<SyncRecoveryError>().is_some() {\n164\t                    tracing::error!(\n165\t                        bead_id = %bead.id,\n166\t                        error = %e,\n167\t                        \"br release failed after SYNC_CONFLICT recovery — \\\n168\t                         worker will continue (bead may remain assigned)\"\n169\t                    );\n170\t                } else {\n171\t                    tracing::error!(\n172\t                        bead_id = %bead.id,\n173\t                        error = %e,\n174\t                        \"br release failed — worker will continue (bead may remain assigned)\"\n175\t                    );\n176\t                }\n177\t                events.push(EventKind::BeadReleaseFailed {\n178\t                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n179\t                    reason: e.to_string(),\n180\t                });\n181\t            }\n182\t            Err(_) => {\n183\t                // Timeout after 30s.\n184\t                tracing::error!(\n185\t                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n186\t                    \"br release timed out after 30s — worker will continue (bead may remain assigned)\"\n187\t                );\n188\t                events.push(EventKind::BeadReleaseFailed {\n189\t                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n190\t                    reason: \"timeout after 30s\".to_string(),\n191\t                });\n192\t            }\n193\t        }\n194\t\n195\t        Ok(events)\n196\t    }\n197\t\n198\t    /// Handle a process output for the given bead.\n199\t    ///\n200\t    /// Uses `classify()` to determine the outcome, then dispatches to the\n201\t    /// per-outcome handler. Every `Outcome` variant has an explicit arm —\n202\t    /// no wildcards. Returns a `HandlerResult` describing what happened.\n203\t    #[tracing::instrument(\n204\t        name = \"bead.outcome\",\n205\t        skip(self, store, bead, output),\n206\t        fields(\n207\t            needle.bead.id = %bead.id,\n208\t            needle.outcome = tracing::field::Empty,\n209\t            needle.outcome.action = tracing::field::Empty,\n210\t        )\n211\t    )]\n212\t    pub async fn handle(\n213\t        &self,\n214\t        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n215\t        bead: &Bead,\n216\t        output: &AgentOutcome,\n217\t        was_interrupted: bool,\n218\t    ) -> Result<HandlerResult> {\n219\t        let outcome = classify(output.exit_code, was_interrupted);\n220\t\n221\t        // Set outcome as span attribute\n222\t        tracing::Span::current().record(\"needle.outcome\", outcome.as_str());\n223\t\n224\t        tracing::info!(\n225\t            bead_id = %bead.id,\n226\t            exit_code = output.exit_code,\n227\t            outcome = %outcome,\n228\t            \"handling agent outcome\"\n229\t        );\n230\t\n231\t        // Use emit_try_lock() to avoid blocking if telemetry writer is stuck.\n232\t        // This prevents worker hang in HANDLING state when telemetry is wedged.\n233\t        let _ = self.telemetry.emit_try_lock(EventKind::OutcomeClassified {\n234\t            bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n235\t            outcome: outcome.as_str().to_string(),\n236\t            exit_code: output.exit_code,\n237\t        });\n238\t\n239\t        let (bead_action, telemetry_events) = match outcome.clone() {\n240\t            Outcome::Success => self.handle_success(store, bead).await?,\n241\t            Outcome::Failure => self.handle_failure(store, bead).await?,\n242\t            Outcome::Timeout => self.handle_timeout(store, bead).await?,\n243\t            Outcome::AgentNotFound => self.handle_agent_not_found(store, bead).await?,\n244\t            Outcome::Interrupted => self.handle_interrupted(store, bead).await?,\n245\t            Outcome::Crash(code) => self.handle_crash(store, bead, code).await?,\n246\t        };\n247\t\n248\t        // Emit sub-handler events (e.g. BeadCompleted, BeadOrphaned) to the\n249\t        // telemetry sink so they appear in the JSONL log.\n250\t        // Use emit_try_lock() to avoid blocking if telemetry writer is stuck.\n251\t        for event in &telemetry_events {\n252\t            let _ = self.telemetry.emit_try_lock(event.clone());\n253\t        }\n254\t\n255\t        // Use emit_try_lock() to avoid blocking if telemetry writer is stuck.\n256\t        let _ = self.telemetry.emit_try_lock(EventKind::OutcomeHandled {\n257\t            bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n258\t            outcome: outcome.as_str().to_string(),\n259\t            action: bead_action.to_string(),\n260\t        });\n261\t\n262\t        // Set action as span attribute\n263\t        tracing::Span::current().record(\"needle.outcome.action\", bead_action.to_string());\n264\t\n265\t        // Set span status: Ok for success, Error otherwise\n266\t        if !matches!(outcome, Outcome::Success) {\n267\t            tracing::Span::current().record(\"otel.status_code\", 2u64);\n268\t            tracing::Span::current().record(\"otel.status_description\", outcome.as_str());\n269\t        }\n270\t\n271\t        Ok(HandlerResult {\n272\t            outcome,\n273\t            bead_action,\n274\t            telemetry_events,\n275\t        })\n276\t    }\n277\t\n278\t    /// Handle a process output with cancellation support.\n279\t    ///\n280\t    /// Checks the cancellation flag before starting the handler and returns\n281\t    /// early if the handler has been cancelled (e.g., due to a timeout in\n282\t    /// the worker). This prevents the handler from making further br calls\n283\t    /// after a timeout has occurred.\n284\t    pub async fn handle_with_cancellation(\n285\t        &self,\n286\t        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n287\t        bead: &Bead,\n288\t        output: &AgentOutcome,\n289\t        was_interrupted: bool,\n290\t        cancelled: Arc<AtomicBool>,\n291\t    ) -> Result<HandlerResult> {\n292\t        // Check if we've been cancelled before starting.\n293\t        if cancelled.load(Ordering::Acquire) {\n294\t            tracing::warn!(\n295\t                bead_id = %bead.id,\n296\t                \"outcome handler cancelled before starting, returning early\"\n297\t            );\n298\t            // Return a default HandlerResult to allow the worker to recover.\n299\t            return Ok(HandlerResult {\n300\t                outcome: classify(output.exit_code, was_interrupted),\n301\t                bead_action: BeadAction::None,\n302\t                telemetry_events: vec![],\n303\t            });\n304\t        }\n305\t\n306\t        // Wrap the handler in a timeout to prevent indefinite hangs.\n307\t        // This is a safety net in case the internal br call timeouts don't work.\n308\t        let bead_id = bead.id.clone();\n309\t        let telemetry = self.telemetry.clone();\n310\t\n311\t        match tokio::time::timeout(\n312\t            std::time::Duration::from_secs(50),\n313\t            self.handle(store, bead, output, was_interrupted),\n314\t        )\n315\t        .await\n316\t        {\n317\t            Ok(Ok(result)) => Ok(result),\n318\t            Ok(Err(e)) => {\n319\t                // Handler returned an error.\n320\t                tracing::error!(\n321\t                    bead_id = %bead_id,\n322\t                    error = %e,\n323\t                    \"outcome handler returned error\"\n324\t                );\n325\t                Err(e)\n326\t            }\n327\t            Err(_) => {\n328\t                // Timeout after 50 seconds.\n329\t                tracing::error!(\n330\t                    bead_id = %bead_id,\n331\t                    \"outcome handler timed out after 50s, returning early to allow worker recovery\"\n332\t                );\n333\t                // Emit a timeout event for observability.\n334\t                let _ = telemetry.emit(EventKind::WorkerHandlingTimeout {\n335\t                    bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n336\t                    outcome: classify(output.exit_code, was_interrupted)\n337\t                        .as_str()\n338\t                        .to_string(),\n339\t                    operation: \"handle\".to_string(),\n340\t                    error: \"timeout after 50s\".to_string(),\n341\t                });\n342\t                // Return a default HandlerResult to allow the worker to recover.\n343\t                // The worker's 60-second timeout wrapper will handle best-effort release.\n344\t                Ok(HandlerResult {\n345\t                    outcome: classify(output.exit_code, was_interrupted),\n346\t                    bead_action: BeadAction::Released,\n347\t                    telemetry_events: vec![EventKind::BeadReleased {\n348\t                        bead_id,\n349\t                        reason: \"handler_timeout\".to_string(),\n350\t                    }],\n351\t                })\n352\t            }\n353\t        }\n354\t    }\n355\t\n356\t    /// Success: run validation gates (if configured), then verify bead closure.\n357\t    ///\n358\t    /// Flow:\n359\t    /// 1. If validation gates are configured (new or legacy format), run them.\n360\t    /// 2. If any gate fails: reopen the bead (if agent closed it) and release it.\n361\t    /// 3. If all gates pass (or none configured): check if agent closed the bead.\n362\t    ///    - Closed → emit BeadCompleted.\n363\t    ///    - Still open → emit BeadOrphaned warning.\n364\t    ///\n365\t    /// NEEDLE does NOT auto-close — the agent owns closure via `br close`.\n366\t    async fn handle_success(\n367\t        &self,\n368\t        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n369\t        bead: &Bead,\n370\t    ) -> Result<(BeadAction, Vec<EventKind>)> {\n371\t        tracing::info!(bead_id = %bead.id, \"agent completed successfully\");\n372\t\n373\t        // Try pluggable gates first, fall back to legacy verification commands.\n374\t        let gate = if !self.config.gates.is_empty() {\n375\t            // New pluggable gate system.\n376\t            let gate_configs: Vec<(String, GateConfig)> = self\n377\t                .config\n378\t                .gates\n379\t                .iter()\n380\t                .enumerate()\n381\t                .map(|(i, config)| (format!(\"gate_{}\", i), config.clone()))\n382\t                .collect();\n383\t            ValidationGate::new(gate_configs, bead.workspace.clone())\n384\t        } else if !self.config.verification.is_empty() {\n385\t            // Legacy verification command format.\n386\t            ValidationGate::from_commands(self.config.verification.clone(), bead.workspace.clone())\n387\t        } else {\n388\t            None\n389\t        };\n390\t\n391\t        if let Some(gate) = gate {\n392\t            let report = gate.run(bead).await?;\n393\t\n394\t            if !report.all_passed {\n395\t                return self.handle_gate_failure(store, bead, &report).await;\n396\t            }\n397\t\n398\t            // All gates passed — emit telemetry.\n399\t            let gates_run = report.results.len() as u32;\n400\t            self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::VerificationPassed {\n401\t                bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n402\t                gates_run,\n403\t            })?;\n404\t            tracing::info!(\n405\t                bead_id = %bead.id,\n406\t                gates_run,\n407\t                \"all validation gates passed\"\n408\t            );\n409\t        }\n410\t\n411\t        // Normal success flow: check if agent closed the bead.\n412\t        let mut events = Vec::new();\n413\t\n414\t        // Use timeout for show() to prevent indefinite hang in HANDLING state.\n415\t        match self.timeout_op(|| store.show(&bead.id), \"show\").await {\n416\t            Ok(Some(current)) if current.status.is_done() => {\n417\t                tracing::info!(bead_id = %bead.id, \"bead confirmed closed by agent\");\n418\t                events.push(EventKind::BeadCompleted {\n419\t                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n420\t                    duration_ms: 0,\n421\t                });\n422\t                // Increment success_count for any skills that matched this bead.\n423\t                if !bead.workspace.as_os_str().is_empty() {\n424\t                    if let Ok(lib) = crate::skill::SkillLibrary::load(&bead.workspace) {\n425\t                        if let Err(e) = lib.increment_success_for_bead(&bead.labels, &bead.title) {\n426\t                            tracing::warn!(\n427\t                                bead_id = %bead.id,\n428\t                                error = %e,\n429\t                                \"failed to increment skill success counts\"\n430\t                            );\n431\t                        }\n432\t                    }\n433\t                }\n434\t            }\n435\t            Ok(Some(current)) => {\n436\t                tracing::warn!(\n437\t                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n438\t                    status = %current.status,\n439\t                    \"agent exited successfully but bead is still open (orphaned)\"\n440\t                );\n441\t                events.push(EventKind::BeadOrphaned {\n442\t                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n443\t                });\n444\t            }\n445\t            Ok(None) => {\n446\t                // Timeout - emit telemetry and continue.\n447\t                tracing::warn!(\n448\t                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n449\t                    \"show() timed out, could not verify bead closure status\"\n450\t                );\n451\t                events.push(EventKind::WorkerHandlingTimeout {\n452\t                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n453\t                    outcome: \"success\".to_string(),\n454\t                    operation: \"show\".to_string(),\n455\t                    error: \"timeout after 30s\".to_string(),\n456\t                });\n457\t            }\n458\t            Err(e) => {\n459\t                tracing::warn!(\n460\t                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n461\t                    error = %e,\n462\t                    \"could not verify bead closure status\"\n463\t                );\n464\t                events.push(EventKind::WorkerHandlingTimeout {\n465\t                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n466\t                    outcome: \"success\".to_string(),\n467\t                    operation: \"show\".to_string(),\n468\t                    error: e.to_string(),\n469\t                });\n470\t            }\n471\t        }\n472\t\n473\t        Ok((BeadAction::None, events))\n474\t    }\n475\t\n476\t    /// Handle gate failure: reopen the bead if it was closed, then release it.\n477\t    async fn handle_gate_failure(\n478\t        &self,\n479\t        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n480\t        bead: &Bead,\n481\t        report: &crate::validation::GateReport,\n482\t    ) -> Result<(BeadAction, Vec<EventKind>)> {\n483\t        // Find the first failing gate for telemetry.\n484\t        let (failed_gate, reason) = report\n485\t            .results\n486\t            .iter()\n487\t            .find(|(_, r)| !r.passed())\n488\t            .map(|(name, r)| (name.clone(), r.failure_reason().unwrap_or(\"\").to_string()))\n489\t            .unwrap_or_else(|| (\"unknown\".to_string(), \"unknown error\".to_string()));\n490\t\n491\t        tracing::warn!(\n492\t            bead_id = %bead.id,\n493\t            gate = %failed_gate,\n494\t            reason = %reason,\n495\t            \"validation gate failed — releasing bead\"\n496\t        );\n497\t\n498\t        // Emit verification failure telemetry.\n499\t        self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::VerificationFailed {\n500\t            bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n501\t            command: failed_gate.clone(),\n502\t            exit_code: None,\n503\t            output: reason,\n504\t        })?;\n505\t\n506\t        let mut events = Vec::new();\n507\t\n508\t        // If the agent already closed the bead, reopen it before releasing.\n509\t        // Use timeout to prevent indefinite hang in HANDLING state.\n510\t        match self.timeout_op(|| store.show(&bead.id), \"show\").await {\n511\t            Ok(Some(current)) if current.status.is_done() => {\n512\t                tracing::info!(\n513\t                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n514\t                    \"reopening bead closed by agent (verification failed)\"\n515\t                );\n516\t                match self.timeout_op(|| store.reopen(&bead.id), \"reopen\").await {\n517\t                    Ok(_) => {}\n518\t                    Err(e) => {\n519\t                        tracing::warn!(\n520\t                            bead_id = %bead.id,\n521\t                            error = %e,\n522\t                            \"failed to reopen bead — attempting release anyway\"\n523\t                        );\n524\t                        events.push(EventKind::WorkerHandlingTimeout {\n525\t                            bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n526\t                            outcome: \"gate_failure\".to_string(),\n527\t                            operation: \"reopen\".to_string(),\n528\t                            error: e.to_string(),\n529\t                        });\n530\t                    }\n531\t                }\n532\t            }\n533\t            Ok(None) => {\n534\t                tracing::warn!(\n535\t                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n536\t                    \"show() timed out during gate failure handling, skipping reopen\"\n537\t                );\n538\t                events.push(EventKind::WorkerHandlingTimeout {\n539\t                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n540\t                    outcome: \"gate_failure\".to_string(),\n541\t                    operation: \"show\".to_string(),\n542\t                    error: \"timeout after 30s\".to_string(),\n543\t                });\n544\t            }\n545\t            Err(e) => {\n546\t                tracing::warn!(\n547\t                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n548\t                    error = %e,\n549\t                    \"show() failed during gate failure handling\"\n550\t                );\n551\t            }\n552\t            _ => {}\n553\t        }\n554\t\n555\t        // Release the bead back to open with flush-before-release and sync recovery.\n556\t        let mut release_events = self.release_bead(store, bead).await?;\n557\t        events.append(&mut release_events);\n558\t\n559\t        // If release succeeded, increment failure count.\n560\t        let release_succeeded = events\n561\t            .iter()\n562\t            .any(|e| matches!(e, EventKind::BeadReleased { .. }));\n563\t        if release_succeeded {\n564\t            if let Err(e) = self.increment_failure_count(store, bead).await {\n565\t                tracing::warn!(\n566\t                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n567\t                    error = %e,\n568\t                    \"failed to increment failure count after gate failure\"\n569\t                );\n570\t            }\n571\t        }\n572\t\n573\t        // Add a label indicating verification failure.\n574\t        if let Err(e) = tokio::time::timeout(\n575\t            std::time::Duration::from_secs(30),\n576\t            store.add_label(&bead.id, \"verification-failed\"),\n577\t        )\n578\t        .await\n579\t        {\n580\t            tracing::warn!(\n581\t                bead_id = %bead.id,\n582\t                error = %e,\n583\t                \"failed to add verification-failed label\"\n584\t            );\n585\t        }\n586\t\n587\t        Ok((BeadAction::Released, events))\n588\t    }\n589\t\n590\t    /// Failure: release bead and increment failure count.\n591\t    ///\n592\t    /// Mitosis evaluation (for multi-task splitting) is handled externally by\n593\t    /// the worker after outcome handling — see `MitosisEvaluator`.\n594\t    ///\n595\t    /// If br calls timeout or fail, logs the error and continues — does not\n596\t    /// block the worker in HANDLING state indefinitely.\n597\t    async fn handle_failure(\n598\t        &self,\n599\t        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n600\t        bead: &Bead,\n601\t    ) -> Result<(BeadAction, Vec<EventKind>)> {\n602\t        tracing::warn!(bead_id = %bead.id, \"agent failure — releasing bead\");\n603\t\n604\t        let mut events = self.release_bead(store, bead).await?;\n605\t\n606\t        // If release succeeded, increment failure count.\n607\t        // Check if the last event is BeadReleased (success) or BeadReleaseFailed (failure).\n608\t        let release_succeeded = events\n609\t            .iter()\n610\t            .any(|e| matches!(e, EventKind::BeadReleased { .. }));\n611\t        if release_succeeded {\n612\t            if let Err(e) = self.increment_failure_count(store, bead).await {\n613\t                tracing::warn!(\n614\t                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n615\t                    error = %e,\n616\t                    \"failed to increment failure count after release\"\n617\t                );\n618\t            }\n619\t        }\n620\t\n621\t        // Ensure we emit a reason event for telemetry.\n622\t        if !events.iter().any(|e| {\n623\t            matches!(\n624\t                e,\n625\t                EventKind::BeadReleased { .. } | EventKind::BeadReleaseFailed { .. }\n626\t            )\n627\t        }) {\n628\t            events.push(EventKind::BeadReleased {\n629\t                bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n630\t                reason: \"failure\".to_string(),\n631\t            });\n632\t        }\n633\t\n634\t        Ok((BeadAction::Released, events))\n635\t    }\n636\t\n637\t    /// Timeout: release bead and add `deferred` label.\n638\t    ///\n639\t    /// If br calls timeout or fail, logs the error and continues — does not\n640\t    /// block the worker in HANDLING state indefinitely.\n641\t    async fn handle_timeout(\n642\t        &self,\n643\t        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n644\t        bead: &Bead,\n645\t    ) -> Result<(BeadAction, Vec<EventKind>)> {\n646\t        tracing::warn!(bead_id = %bead.id, \"agent timed out — releasing bead as deferred\");\n647\t\n648\t        let mut events = self.release_bead(store, bead).await?;\n649\t\n650\t        // If release succeeded, add deferred label.\n651\t        let release_succeeded = events\n652\t            .iter()\n653\t            .any(|e| matches!(e, EventKind::BeadReleased { .. }));\n654\t        if release_succeeded {\n655\t            if let Err(e) = tokio::time::timeout(\n656\t                std::time::Duration::from_secs(30),\n657\t                store.add_label(&bead.id, \"deferred\"),\n658\t            )\n659\t            .await\n660\t            {\n661\t                tracing::warn!(\n662\t                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n663\t                    error = %e,\n664\t                    \"add_label deferred timed out or failed after timeout release\"\n665\t                );\n666\t                events.push(EventKind::WorkerHandlingTimeout {\n667\t                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n668\t                    outcome: \"timeout\".to_string(),\n669\t                    operation: \"add_label\".to_string(),\n670\t                    error: e.to_string(),\n671\t                });\n672\t            }\n673\t        }\n674\t\n675\t        Ok((BeadAction::Deferred, events))\n676\t    }\n677\t\n678\t    /// Crash: release bead and create alert bead with diagnostic info.\n679\t    async fn handle_crash(\n680\t        &self,\n681\t        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n682\t        bead: &Bead,\n683\t        signal_code: i32,\n684\t    ) -> Result<(BeadAction, Vec<EventKind>)> {\n685\t        tracing::error!(\n686\t            bead_id = %bead.id,\n687\t            signal_code,\n688\t            agent = %self.config.agent.default,\n689\t            \"agent crashed — releasing bead and creating alert\"\n690\t        );\n691\t\n692\t        let events = self.release_bead(store, bead).await?;\n693\t\n694\t        // Create alert bead with diagnostic info (best-effort).\n695\t        let signal_num = if signal_code > 128 {\n696\t            signal_code - 128\n697\t        } else {\n698\t            signal_code\n699\t        };\n700\t        let timestamp = Utc::now().to_rfc3339();\n701\t        let alert_title = format!(\"ALERT: Agent crash on bead {}\", bead.id);\n702\t        let alert_body = format!(\n703\t            \"## Agent Crash Report\\n\\\n704\t             \\n\\\n705\t             - **Bead ID**: {}\\n\\\n706\t             - **Agent**: {}\\n\\\n707\t             - **Exit code**: {} (signal {})\\n\\\n708\t             - **Workspace**: {}\\n\\\n709\t             - **Timestamp**: {}\\n\\\n710\t             \\n\\\n711\t             The agent process was killed. This bead has been released for retry.\",\n712\t            bead.id,\n713\t            self.config.agent.default,\n714\t            signal_code,\n715\t            signal_num,\n716\t            bead.workspace.display(),\n717\t            timestamp,\n718\t        );\n719\t\n720\t        // Hook 4: propagate stitch labels from the crashed bead to the alert.\n721\t        let signal_label = format!(\"signal-{}\", signal_num);\n722\t        let mut alert_labels: Vec<String> = vec![\"alert\".into(), \"crash\".into(), signal_label];\n723\t        alert_labels.extend(crate::types::extract_stitch_labels(&bead.labels));\n724\t        let alert_label_refs: Vec<&str> = alert_labels.iter().map(|s| s.as_str()).collect();\n725\t        match tokio::time::timeout(\n726\t            std::time::Duration::from_secs(30),\n727\t            store.create_bead(&alert_title, &alert_body, &alert_label_refs),\n728\t        )\n729\t        .await\n730\t        {\n731\t            Ok(Ok(alert_id)) => {\n732\t                tracing::info!(\n733\t                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n734\t                    %alert_id,\n735\t                    \"crash alert bead created\"\n736\t                );\n737\t            }\n738\t            Ok(Err(e)) => {\n739\t                tracing::warn!(\n740\t                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n741\t                    error = %e,\n742\t                    \"failed to create crash alert bead\"\n743\t                );\n744\t            }\n745\t            Err(_) => {\n746\t                tracing::warn!(\n747\t                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n748\t                    \"create_bead timed out after 30s during crash handling\"\n749\t                );\n750\t            }\n751\t        }\n752\t\n753\t        Ok((BeadAction::Alerted, events))\n754\t    }\n755\t\n756\t    /// AgentNotFound: release bead, emit error. No retry — this is a config issue.\n757\t    async fn handle_agent_not_found(\n758\t        &self,\n759\t        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n760\t        bead: &Bead,\n761\t    ) -> Result<(BeadAction, Vec<EventKind>)> {\n762\t        tracing::error!(\n763\t            bead_id = %bead.id,\n764\t            agent = %self.config.agent.default,\n765\t            \"agent binary not found — releasing bead (config issue, no retry)\"\n766\t        );\n767\t\n768\t        let events = self.release_bead(store, bead).await?;\n769\t        Ok((BeadAction::Released, events))\n770\t    }\n771\t\n772\t    /// Interrupted: release bead for graceful shutdown.\n773\t    async fn handle_interrupted(\n774\t        &self,\n775\t        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n776\t        bead: &Bead,\n777\t    ) -> Result<(BeadAction, Vec<EventKind>)> {\n778\t        tracing::info!(bead_id = %bead.id, \"agent interrupted — releasing bead for clean shutdown\");\n779\t\n780\t        let events = self.release_bead(store, bead).await?;\n781\t        Ok((BeadAction::Released, events))\n782\t    }\n783\t\n784\t    /// Increment the failure count label on a bead.\n785\t    ///\n786\t    /// Labels follow the pattern `failure-count:N`. If `failure-count:2` exists,\n787\t    /// the old label is removed and `failure-count:3` is added.\n788\t    ///\n789\t    /// All `br` calls are wrapped in timeouts to prevent indefinite hang in\n790\t    /// HANDLING state. Failures are non-fatal — we log and continue.\n791\t    async fn increment_failure_count(&self, store: &dyn BeadStore, bead: &Bead) -> Result<()> {\n792\t        // Read labels with timeout.\n793\t        let labels =\n794\t            match tokio::time::timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(30), store.labels(&bead.id))\n795\t                .await\n796\t            {\n797\t                Ok(Ok(l)) => l,\n798\t                Ok(Err(e)) => {\n799\t                    tracing::warn!(\n800\t                        bead_id = %bead.id,\n801\t                        error = %e,\n802\t                        \"could not read labels to increment failure count\"\n803\t                    );\n804\t                    return Ok(());\n805\t                }\n806\t                Err(_) => {\n807\t                    tracing::warn!(\n808\t                        bead_id = %bead.id,\n809\t                        \"labels() timed out after 30s, skipping failure count increment\"\n810\t                    );\n811\t                    return Ok(());\n812\t                }\n813\t            };\n814\t\n815\t        let current_count = labels\n816\t            .iter()\n817\t            .filter_map(|l| l.strip_prefix(\"failure-count:\"))\n818\t            .filter_map(|n| n.parse::<u32>().ok())\n819\t            .max()\n820\t            .unwrap_or(0);\n821\t\n822\t        let new_count = current_count + 1;\n823\t        let new_label = format!(\"failure-count:{}\", new_count);\n824\t\n825\t        // Remove old failure-count labels before adding the new one.\n826\t        // Each remove_label call is wrapped in a timeout.\n827\t        for label in &labels {\n828\t            if label.starts_with(\"failure-count:\") {\n829\t                match tokio::time::timeout(\n830\t                    std::time::Duration::from_secs(30),\n831\t                    store.remove_label(&bead.id, label),\n832\t                )\n833\t                .await\n834\t                {\n835\t                    Ok(Ok(())) => {}\n836\t                    Ok(Err(e)) => {\n837\t                        tracing::warn!(\n838\t                            bead_id = %bead.id,\n839\t                            label,\n840\t                            error = %e,\n841\t                            \"failed to remove old failure-count label\"\n842\t                        );\n843\t                    }\n844\t                    Err(_) => {\n845\t                        tracing::warn!(\n846\t                            bead_id = %bead.id,\n847\t                            label,\n848\t                            \"remove_label timed out after 30s\"\n849\t                        );\n850\t                    }\n851\t                }\n852\t            }\n853\t        }\n854\t\n855\t        // Add the new label with timeout.\n856\t        match tokio::time::timeout(\n857\t            std::time::Duration::from_secs(30),\n858\t            store.add_label(&bead.id, &new_label),\n859\t        )\n860\t        .await\n861\t        {\n862\t            Ok(Ok(())) => {\n863\t                tracing::debug!(\n864\t                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n865\t                    count = new_count,\n866\t                    \"failure count incremented\"\n867\t                );\n868\t            }\n869\t            Ok(Err(e)) => {\n870\t                tracing::warn!(\n871\t                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n872\t                    error = %e,\n873\t                    \"failed to add failure-count label\"\n874\t                );\n875\t            }\n876\t            Err(_) => {\n877\t                tracing::warn!(\n878\t                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n879\t                    \"add_label timed out after 30s\"\n880\t                );\n881\t            }\n882\t        }\n883\t\n884\t        Ok(())\n885\t    }\n886\t}\n887\t\n888\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n889\t// Outcome Display\n890\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n891\t\n892\timpl fmt::Display for Outcome {\n893\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n894\t        match self {\n895\t            Outcome::Success => write!(f, \"Success\"),\n896\t            Outcome::Failure => write!(f, \"Failure\"),\n897\t            Outcome::Timeout => write!(f, \"Timeout\"),\n898\t            Outcome::AgentNotFound => write!(f, \"AgentNotFound\"),\n899\t            Outcome::Interrupted => write!(f, \"Interrupted\"),\n900\t            Outcome::Crash(code) => write!(f, \"Crash({})\", code),\n901\t        }\n902\t    }\n903\t}\n904\t\n905\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n906\t// Tests\n907\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n908\t\n909\t#[cfg(test)]\n910\tmod tests {\n911\t    use super::*;\n912\t    use crate::bead_store::Filters;\n913\t
