Outcome routing: map agent exit codes to explicit handlers.\n//!\n//! Every possible exit code has a named handler. The type system enforces\n//! exhaustiveness — if an outcome can happen, it must have a handler.\n//!\n//! Depends on: `types`, `config`, `bead_store`, `telemetry`, `validation`.\n\nuse std::fmt;\nuse std::sync::atomic::{AtomicBool, Ordering};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\n\nuse anyhow::Result;\nuse chrono::Utc;\n\nuse crate::bead_store::{BeadStore, SyncRecoveryError};\nuse crate::config::Config;\nuse crate::telemetry::{EventKind, Telemetry};\n#[cfg(test)]\nuse crate::types::BeadStatus;\nuse crate::types::{AgentOutcome, Bead, BeadAction, HandlerResult, Outcome};\nuse crate::validation::{GateConfig, ValidationGate};\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// classify (convenience re-export)\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Classify an agent exit code into an `Outcome`, with shutdown signal support.\n///\n/// Delegates to `Outcome::classify()`. Provided here for ergonomic imports.\npub fn classify(exit_code: i32, was_interrupted: bool) -> Outcome {\n    Outcome::classify(exit_code, was_interrupted)\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// OutcomeHandler\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Routes agent outcomes to their explicit handlers.\npub struct OutcomeHandler {\n    config: Config,\n    telemetry: Telemetry,\n}\n\nimpl OutcomeHandler {\n    pub fn new(config: Config, telemetry: Telemetry) -> Self {\n        OutcomeHandler { config, telemetry }\n    }\n\n    /// Run a bead store operation with a 30s timeout.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Ok(Some(T))` on success, `Ok(None)` on timeout, and `Err(E)` on\n    /// other errors. Callers should treat timeout and error as non-fatal — log\n    /// and continue rather than blocking the worker in HANDLING state.\n    async fn timeout_op<T, F, Fut>(\n        &self,\n        op: F,\n        operation_name: &str,\n    ) -> Result<Option<T>, anyhow::Error>\n    where\n        F: FnOnce() -> Fut,\n        Fut: std::future::Future<Output = Result<T, anyhow::Error>>,\n    {\n        match tokio::time::timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(30), op()).await {\n            Ok(Ok(result)) => Ok(Some(result)),\n            Ok(Err(e)) => Err(e),\n            Err(_) => {\n                tracing::error!(\n                    operation = operation_name,\n                    \"bead store operation timed out after 30s\"\n                );\n                Ok(None)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Release a bead with sync conflict recovery and graceful failure handling.\n    ///\n    /// Flow:\n    /// 1. Run `br sync --flush-only` to push local writes to JSONL first.\n    /// 2. Attempt `br update` to release the bead (set status=open, assignee=\"\").\n    /// 3. If SYNC_CONFLICT recovery fails, emit `BeadReleaseFailed` and return Ok.\n    ///\n    /// This method never returns an error — release failures are non-fatal.\n    /// The worker can continue processing other beads even if release fails.\n    async fn release_bead(&self, store: &dyn BeadStore, bead: &Bead) -> Result<Vec<EventKind>> {\n        let mut events = Vec::new();\n\n        // Step 1: Flush local writes to JSONL before attempting release.\n        // Emit heartbeat before br call to track where hang occurs.\n        let _ = self.telemetry.emit_try_lock(EventKind::HeartbeatEmitted {\n            bead_id: Some(bead.id.clone()),\n            state: \"HANDLING_FLUSH\".to_string(),\n        });\n\n        match self.timeout_op(|| store.flush(), \"flush\").await {\n            Ok(Some(())) => {\n                tracing::debug!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    \"flushed local changes to JSONL before release\"\n                );\n                // Emit heartbeat after successful flush.\n                let _ = self.telemetry.emit_try_lock(EventKind::HeartbeatEmitted {\n                    bead_id: Some(bead.id.clone()),\n                    state: \"HANDLING_FLUSH_DONE\".to_string(),\n                });\n            }\n            Ok(None) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    \"flush timed out before release, attempting release anyway\"\n                );\n                events.push(EventKind::WorkerHandlingTimeout {\n                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n                    outcome: \"release\".to_string(),\n                    operation: \"flush\".to_string(),\n                    error: \"timeout after 30s\".to_string(),\n                });\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"flush failed before release, attempting release anyway\"\n                );\n                events.push(EventKind::WorkerHandlingTimeout {\n                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n                    outcome: \"release\".to_string(),\n                    operation: \"flush\".to_string(),\n                    error: e.to_string(),\n                });\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Step 2: Attempt release with timeout protection.\n        // Emit heartbeat before br release call to track where hang occurs.\n        let _ = self.telemetry.emit_try_lock(EventKind::HeartbeatEmitted {\n            bead_id: Some(bead.id.clone()),\n            state: \"HANDLING_RELEASE\".to_string(),\n        });\n\n        let release_result =\n            tokio::time::timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(30), store.release(&bead.id)).await;\n\n        match release_result {\n            Ok(Ok(())) => {\n                tracing::debug!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    \"bead released successfully\"\n                );\n                // Emit heartbeat after successful release.\n                let _ = self.telemetry.emit_try_lock(EventKind::HeartbeatEmitted {\n                    bead_id: Some(bead.id.clone()),\n                    state: \"HANDLING_RELEASE_DONE\".to_string(),\n                });\n                events.push(EventKind::BeadReleased {\n                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n                    reason: \"release_success\".to_string(),\n                });\n            }\n            Ok(Err(e)) => {\n                // Release failed (br error, not timeout).\n                // Check if this is a SyncRecoveryError (sync recovery was attempted but failed).\n                if e.downcast_ref::<SyncRecoveryError>().is_some() {\n                    tracing::error!(\n                        bead_id = %bead.id,\n                        error = %e,\n                        \"br release failed after SYNC_CONFLICT recovery — \\\n                         worker will continue (bead may remain assigned)\"\n                    );\n                } else {\n                    tracing::error!(\n                        bead_id = %bead.id,\n                        error = %e,\n                        \"br release failed — worker will continue (bead may remain assigned)\"\n                    );\n                }\n                events.push(EventKind::BeadReleaseFailed {\n                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n                    reason: e.to_string(),\n                });\n            }\n            Err(_) => {\n                // Timeout after 30s.\n                tracing::error!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    \"br release timed out after 30s — worker will continue (bead may remain assigned)\"\n                );\n                events.push(EventKind::BeadReleaseFailed {\n                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n                    reason: \"timeout after 30s\".to_string(),\n                });\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(events)\n    }\n\n    /// Handle a process output for the given bead.\n    ///\n    /// Uses `classify()` to determine the outcome, then dispatches to the\n    /// per-outcome handler. Every `Outcome` variant has an explicit arm —\n    /// no wildcards. Returns a `HandlerResult` describing what happened.\n    #[tracing::instrument(\n        name = \"bead.outcome\",\n        skip(self, store, bead, output),\n        fields(\n            needle.bead.id = %bead.id,\n            needle.outcome = tracing::field::Empty,\n            needle.outcome.action = tracing::field::Empty,\n        )\n    )]\n    pub async fn handle(\n        &self,\n        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n        bead: &Bead,\n        output: &AgentOutcome,\n        was_interrupted: bool,\n    ) -> Result<HandlerResult> {\n        let outcome = classify(output.exit_code, was_interrupted);\n\n        // Set outcome as span attribute\n        tracing::Span::current().record(\"needle.outcome\", outcome.as_str());\n\n        tracing::info!(\n            bead_id = %bead.id,\n            exit_code = output.exit_code,\n            outcome = %outcome,\n            \"handling agent outcome\"\n        );\n\n        // Use emit_try_lock() to avoid blocking if telemetry writer is stuck.\n        // This prevents worker hang in HANDLING state when telemetry is wedged.\n        let _ = self.telemetry.emit_try_lock(EventKind::OutcomeClassified {\n            bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n            outcome: outcome.as_str().to_string(),\n            exit_code: output.exit_code,\n        });\n\n        let (bead_action, telemetry_events) = match outcome.clone() {\n            Outcome::Success => self.handle_success(store, bead).await?,\n            Outcome::Failure => self.handle_failure(store, bead).await?,\n            Outcome::Timeout => self.handle_timeout(store, bead).await?,\n            Outcome::AgentNotFound => self.handle_agent_not_found(store, bead).await?,\n            Outcome::Interrupted => self.handle_interrupted(store, bead).await?,\n            Outcome::Crash(code) => self.handle_crash(store, bead, code).await?,\n        };\n\n        // Emit sub-handler events (e.g. BeadCompleted, BeadOrphaned) to the\n        // telemetry sink so they appear in the JSONL log.\n        // Use emit_try_lock() to avoid blocking if telemetry writer is stuck.\n        for event in &telemetry_events {\n            let _ = self.telemetry.emit_try_lock(event.clone());\n        }\n\n        // Use emit_try_lock() to avoid blocking if telemetry writer is stuck.\n        let _ = self.telemetry.emit_try_lock(EventKind::OutcomeHandled {\n            bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n            outcome: outcome.as_str().to_string(),\n            action: bead_action.to_string(),\n        });\n\n        // Set action as span attribute\n        tracing::Span::current().record(\"needle.outcome.action\", bead_action.to_string());\n\n        // Set span status: Ok for success, Error otherwise\n        if !matches!(outcome, Outcome::Success) {\n            tracing::Span::current().record(\"otel.status_code\", 2u64);\n            tracing::Span::current().record(\"otel.status_description\", outcome.as_str());\n        }\n\n        Ok(HandlerResult {\n            outcome,\n            bead_action,\n            telemetry_events,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Handle a process output with cancellation support.\n    ///\n    /// Checks the cancellation flag before starting the handler and returns\n    /// early if the handler has been cancelled (e.g., due to a timeout in\n    /// the worker). This prevents the handler from making further br calls\n    /// after a timeout has occurred.\n    pub async fn handle_with_cancellation(\n        &self,\n        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n        bead: &Bead,\n        output: &AgentOutcome,\n        was_interrupted: bool,\n        cancelled: Arc<AtomicBool>,\n    ) -> Result<HandlerResult> {\n        // Check if we've been cancelled before starting.\n        if cancelled.load(Ordering::Acquire) {\n            tracing::warn!(\n                bead_id = %bead.id,\n                \"outcome handler cancelled before starting, returning early\"\n            );\n            // Return a default HandlerResult to allow the worker to recover.\n            return Ok(HandlerResult {\n                outcome: classify(output.exit_code, was_interrupted),\n                bead_action: BeadAction::None,\n                telemetry_events: vec![],\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Wrap the handler in a timeout to prevent indefinite hangs.\n        // This is a safety net in case the internal br call timeouts don't work.\n        let bead_id = bead.id.clone();\n        let telemetry = self.telemetry.clone();\n\n        match tokio::time::timeout(\n            std::time::Duration::from_secs(50),\n            self.handle(store, bead, output, was_interrupted),\n        )\n        .await\n        {\n            Ok(Ok(result)) => Ok(result),\n            Ok(Err(e)) => {\n                // Handler returned an error.\n                tracing::error!(\n                    bead_id = %bead_id,\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"outcome handler returned error\"\n                );\n                Err(e)\n            }\n            Err(_) => {\n                // Timeout after 50 seconds.\n                tracing::error!(\n                    bead_id = %bead_id,\n                    \"outcome handler timed out after 50s, returning early to allow worker recovery\"\n                );\n                // Emit a timeout event for observability.\n                let _ = telemetry.emit(EventKind::WorkerHandlingTimeout {\n                    bead_id: bead_id.clone(),\n                    outcome: classify(output.exit_code, was_interrupted)\n                        .as_str()\n                        .to_string(),\n                    operation: \"handle\".to_string(),\n                    error: \"timeout after 50s\".to_string(),\n                });\n                // Return a default HandlerResult to allow the worker to recover.\n                // The worker's 60-second timeout wrapper will handle best-effort release.\n                Ok(HandlerResult {\n                    outcome: classify(output.exit_code, was_interrupted),\n                    bead_action: BeadAction::Released,\n                    telemetry_events: vec![EventKind::BeadReleased {\n                        bead_id,\n                        reason: \"handler_timeout\".to_string(),\n                    }],\n                })\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Success: run validation gates (if configured), then verify bead closure.\n    ///\n    /// Flow:\n    /// 1. If validation gates are configured (new or legacy format), run them.\n    /// 2. If any gate fails: reopen the bead (if agent closed it) and release it.\n    /// 3. If all gates pass (or none configured): check if agent closed the bead.\n    ///    - Closed → emit BeadCompleted.\n    ///    - Still open → emit BeadOrphaned warning.\n    ///\n    /// NEEDLE does NOT auto-close — the agent owns closure via `br close`.\n    async fn handle_success(\n        &self,\n        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n        bead: &Bead,\n    ) -> Result<(BeadAction, Vec<EventKind>)> {\n        tracing::info!(bead_id = %bead.id, \"agent completed successfully\");\n\n        // Try pluggable gates first, fall back to legacy verification commands.\n        let gate = if !self.config.gates.is_empty() {\n            // New pluggable gate system.\n            let gate_configs: Vec<(String, GateConfig)> = self\n                .config\n                .gates\n                .iter()\n                .enumerate()\n                .map(|(i, config)| (format!(\"gate_{}\", i), config.clone()))\n                .collect();\n            ValidationGate::new(gate_configs, bead.workspace.clone())\n        } else if !self.config.verification.is_empty() {\n            // Legacy verification command format.\n            ValidationGate::from_commands(self.config.verification.clone(), bead.workspace.clone())\n        } else {\n            None\n        };\n\n        if let Some(gate) = gate {\n            let report = gate.run(bead).await?;\n\n            if !report.all_passed {\n                return self.handle_gate_failure(store, bead, &report).await;\n            }\n\n            // All gates passed — emit telemetry.\n            let gates_run = report.results.len() as u32;\n            self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::VerificationPassed {\n                bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n                gates_run,\n            })?;\n            tracing::info!(\n                bead_id = %bead.id,\n                gates_run,\n                \"all validation gates passed\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        // Normal success flow: check if agent closed the bead.\n        let mut events = Vec::new();\n\n        // Use timeout for show() to prevent indefinite hang in HANDLING state.\n        match self.timeout_op(|| store.show(&bead.id), \"show\").await {\n            Ok(Some(current)) if current.status.is_done() => {\n                tracing::info!(bead_id = %bead.id, \"bead confirmed closed by agent\");\n                events.push(EventKind::BeadCompleted {\n                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n                    duration_ms: 0,\n                });\n                // Increment success_count for any skills that matched this bead.\n                if !bead.workspace.as_os_str().is_empty() {\n                    if let Ok(lib) = crate::skill::SkillLibrary::load(&bead.workspace) {\n                        if let Err(e) = lib.increment_success_for_bead(&bead.labels, &bead.title) {\n                            tracing::warn!(\n                                bead_id = %bead.id,\n                                error = %e,\n                                \"failed to increment skill success counts\"\n                            );\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            Ok(Some(current)) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    status = %current.status,\n                    \"agent exited successfully but bead is still open (orphaned)\"\n                );\n                events.push(EventKind::BeadOrphaned {\n                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n                });\n            }\n            Ok(None) => {\n                // Timeout - emit telemetry and continue.\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    \"show() timed out, could not verify bead closure status\"\n                );\n                events.push(EventKind::WorkerHandlingTimeout {\n                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n                    outcome: \"success\".to_string(),\n                    operation: \"show\".to_string(),\n                    error: \"timeout after 30s\".to_string(),\n                });\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"could not verify bead closure status\"\n                );\n                events.push(EventKind::WorkerHandlingTimeout {\n                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n                    outcome: \"success\".to_string(),\n                    operation: \"show\".to_string(),\n                    error: e.to_string(),\n                });\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok((BeadAction::None, events))\n    }\n\n    /// Handle gate failure: reopen the bead if it was closed, then release it.\n    async fn handle_gate_failure(\n        &self,\n        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n        bead: &Bead,\n        report: &crate::validation::GateReport,\n    ) -> Result<(BeadAction, Vec<EventKind>)> {\n        // Find the first failing gate for telemetry.\n        let (failed_gate, reason) = report\n            .results\n            .iter()\n            .find(|(_, r)| !r.passed())\n            .map(|(name, r)| (name.clone(), r.failure_reason().unwrap_or(\"\").to_string()))\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| (\"unknown\".to_string(), \"unknown error\".to_string()));\n\n        tracing::warn!(\n            bead_id = %bead.id,\n            gate = %failed_gate,\n            reason = %reason,\n            \"validation gate failed — releasing bead\"\n        );\n\n        // Emit verification failure telemetry.\n        self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::VerificationFailed {\n            bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n            command: failed_gate.clone(),\n            exit_code: None,\n            output: reason,\n        })?;\n\n        let mut events = Vec::new();\n\n        // If the agent already closed the bead, reopen it before releasing.\n        // Use timeout to prevent indefinite hang in HANDLING state.\n        match self.timeout_op(|| store.show(&bead.id), \"show\").await {\n            Ok(Some(current)) if current.status.is_done() => {\n                tracing::info!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    \"reopening bead closed by agent (verification failed)\"\n                );\n                match self.timeout_op(|| store.reopen(&bead.id), \"reopen\").await {\n                    Ok(_) => {}\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        tracing::warn!(\n                            bead_id = %bead.id,\n                            error = %e,\n                            \"failed to reopen bead — attempting release anyway\"\n                        );\n                        events.push(EventKind::WorkerHandlingTimeout {\n                            bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n                            outcome: \"gate_failure\".to_string(),\n                            operation: \"reopen\".to_string(),\n                            error: e.to_string(),\n                        });\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            Ok(None) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    \"show() timed out during gate failure handling, skipping reopen\"\n                );\n                events.push(EventKind::WorkerHandlingTimeout {\n                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n                    outcome: \"gate_failure\".to_string(),\n                    operation: \"show\".to_string(),\n                    error: \"timeout after 30s\".to_string(),\n                });\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"show() failed during gate failure handling\"\n                );\n            }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n\n        // Release the bead back to open with flush-before-release and sync recovery.\n        let mut release_events = self.release_bead(store, bead).await?;\n        events.append(&mut release_events);\n\n        // If release succeeded, increment failure count.\n        let release_succeeded = events\n            .iter()\n            .any(|e| matches!(e, EventKind::BeadReleased { .. }));\n        if release_succeeded {\n            if let Err(e) = self.increment_failure_count(store, bead).await {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"failed to increment failure count after gate failure\"\n                );\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Add a label indicating verification failure.\n        if let Err(e) = tokio::time::timeout(\n            std::time::Duration::from_secs(30),\n            store.add_label(&bead.id, \"verification-failed\"),\n        )\n        .await\n        {\n            tracing::warn!(\n                bead_id = %bead.id,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to add verification-failed label\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        Ok((BeadAction::Released, events))\n    }\n\n    /// Failure: release bead and increment failure count.\n    ///\n    /// Mitosis evaluation (for multi-task splitting) is handled externally by\n    /// the worker after outcome handling — see `MitosisEvaluator`.\n    ///\n    /// If br calls timeout or fail, logs the error and continues — does not\n    /// block the worker in HANDLING state indefinitely.\n    async fn handle_failure(\n        &self,\n        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n        bead: &Bead,\n    ) -> Result<(BeadAction, Vec<EventKind>)> {\n        tracing::warn!(bead_id = %bead.id, \"agent failure — releasing bead\");\n\n        let mut events = self.release_bead(store, bead).await?;\n\n        // If release succeeded, increment failure count.\n        // Check if the last event is BeadReleased (success) or BeadReleaseFailed (failure).\n        let release_succeeded = events\n            .iter()\n            .any(|e| matches!(e, EventKind::BeadReleased { .. }));\n        if release_succeeded {\n            if let Err(e) = self.increment_failure_count(store, bead).await {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"failed to increment failure count after release\"\n                );\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Ensure we emit a reason event for telemetry.\n        if !events.iter().any(|e| {\n            matches!(\n                e,\n                EventKind::BeadReleased { .. } | EventKind::BeadReleaseFailed { .. }\n            )\n        }) {\n            events.push(EventKind::BeadReleased {\n                bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n                reason: \"failure\".to_string(),\n            });\n        }\n\n        Ok((BeadAction::Released, events))\n    }\n\n    /// Timeout: release bead and add `deferred` label.\n    ///\n    /// If br calls timeout or fail, logs the error and continues — does not\n    /// block the worker in HANDLING state indefinitely.\n    async fn handle_timeout(\n        &self,\n        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n        bead: &Bead,\n    ) -> Result<(BeadAction, Vec<EventKind>)> {\n        tracing::warn!(bead_id = %bead.id, \"agent timed out — releasing bead as deferred\");\n\n        let mut events = self.release_bead(store, bead).await?;\n\n        // If release succeeded, add deferred label.\n        let release_succeeded = events\n            .iter()\n            .any(|e| matches!(e, EventKind::BeadReleased { .. }));\n        if release_succeeded {\n            if let Err(e) = tokio::time::timeout(\n                std::time::Duration::from_secs(30),\n                store.add_label(&bead.id, \"deferred\"),\n            )\n            .await\n            {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"add_label deferred timed out or failed after timeout release\"\n                );\n                events.push(EventKind::WorkerHandlingTimeout {\n                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n                    outcome: \"timeout\".to_string(),\n                    operation: \"add_label\".to_string(),\n                    error: e.to_string(),\n                });\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok((BeadAction::Deferred, events))\n    }\n\n    /// Crash: release bead and create alert bead with diagnostic info.\n    async fn handle_crash(\n        &self,\n        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n        bead: &Bead,\n        signal_code: i32,\n    ) -> Result<(BeadAction, Vec<EventKind>)> {\n        tracing::error!(\n            bead_id = %bead.id,\n            signal_code,\n            agent = %self.config.agent.default,\n            \"agent crashed — releasing bead and creating alert\"\n        );\n\n        let events = self.release_bead(store, bead).await?;\n\n        // Create alert bead with diagnostic info (best-effort).\n        let signal_num = if signal_code > 128 {\n            signal_code - 128\n        } else {\n            signal_code\n        };\n        let timestamp = Utc::now().to_rfc3339();\n        let alert_title = format!(\"ALERT: Agent crash on bead {}\", bead.id);\n        let alert_body = format!(\n            \"## Agent Crash Report\\n\\\n             \\n\\\n             - **Bead ID**: {}\\n\\\n             - **Agent**: {}\\n\\\n             - **Exit code**: {} (signal {})\\n\\\n             - **Workspace**: {}\\n\\\n             - **Timestamp**: {}\\n\\\n             \\n\\\n             The agent process was killed. This bead has been released for retry.\",\n            bead.id,\n            self.config.agent.default,\n            signal_code,\n            signal_num,\n            bead.workspace.display(),\n            timestamp,\n        );\n\n        // Hook 4: propagate stitch labels from the crashed bead to the alert.\n        let signal_label = format!(\"signal-{}\", signal_num);\n        let mut alert_labels: Vec<String> = vec![\"alert\".into(), \"crash\".into(), signal_label];\n        alert_labels.extend(crate::types::extract_stitch_labels(&bead.labels));\n        let alert_label_refs: Vec<&str> = alert_labels.iter().map(|s| s.as_str()).collect();\n        match tokio::time::timeout(\n            std::time::Duration::from_secs(30),\n            store.create_bead(&alert_title, &alert_body, &alert_label_refs),\n        )\n        .await\n        {\n            Ok(Ok(alert_id)) => {\n                tracing::info!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    %alert_id,\n                    \"crash alert bead created\"\n                );\n            }\n            Ok(Err(e)) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"failed to create crash alert bead\"\n                );\n            }\n            Err(_) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    \"create_bead timed out after 30s during crash handling\"\n                );\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok((BeadAction::Alerted, events))\n    }\n\n    /// AgentNotFound: release bead, emit error. No retry — this is a config issue.\n    async fn handle_agent_not_found(\n        &self,\n        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n        bead: &Bead,\n    ) -> Result<(BeadAction, Vec<EventKind>)> {\n        tracing::error!(\n            bead_id = %bead.id,\n            agent = %self.config.agent.default,\n            \"agent binary not found — releasing bead (config issue, no retry)\"\n        );\n\n        let events = self.release_bead(store, bead).await?;\n        Ok((BeadAction::Released, events))\n    }\n\n    /// Interrupted: release bead for graceful shutdown.\n    async fn handle_interrupted(\n        &self,\n        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n        bead: &Bead,\n    ) -> Result<(BeadAction, Vec<EventKind>)> {\n        tracing::info!(bead_id = %bead.id, \"agent interrupted — releasing bead for clean shutdown\");\n\n        let events = self.release_bead(store, bead).await?;\n        Ok((BeadAction::Released, events))\n    }\n\n    /// Increment the failure count label on a bead.\n    ///\n    /// Labels follow the pattern `failure-count:N`. If `failure-count:2` exists,\n    /// the old label is removed and `failure-count:3` is added.\n    ///\n    /// All `br` calls are wrapped in timeouts to prevent indefinite hang in\n    /// HANDLING state. Failures are non-fatal — we log and continue.\n    async fn increment_failure_count(&self, store: &dyn BeadStore, bead: &Bead) -> Result<()> {\n        // Read labels with timeout.\n        let labels =\n            match tokio::time::timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(30), store.labels(&bead.id))\n                .await\n            {\n                Ok(Ok(l)) => l,\n                Ok(Err(e)) => {\n                    tracing::warn!(\n                        bead_id = %bead.id,\n                        error = %e,\n                        \"could not read labels to increment failure count\"\n                    );\n                    return Ok(());\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    tracing::warn!(\n                        bead_id = %bead.id,\n                        \"labels() timed out after 30s, skipping failure count increment\"\n                    );\n                    return Ok(());\n                }\n            };\n\n        let current_count = labels\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|l| l.strip_prefix(\"failure-count:\"))\n            .filter_map(|n| n.parse::<u32>().ok())\n            .max()\n            .unwrap_or(0);\n\n        let new_count = current_count + 1;\n        let new_label = format!(\"failure-count:{}\", new_count);\n\n        // Remove old failure-count labels before adding the new one.\n        // Each remove_label call is wrapped in a timeout.\n        for label in &labels {\n            if label.starts_with(\"failure-count:\") {\n                match tokio::time::timeout(\n                    std::time::Duration::from_secs(30),\n                    store.remove_label(&bead.id, label),\n                )\n                .await\n                {\n                    Ok(Ok(())) => {}\n                    Ok(Err(e)) => {\n                        tracing::warn!(\n                            bead_id = %bead.id,\n                            label,\n                            error = %e,\n                            \"failed to remove old failure-count label\"\n                        );\n                    }\n                    Err(_) => {\n                        tracing::warn!(\n                            bead_id = %bead.id,\n                            label,\n                            \"remove_label timed out after 30s\"\n                        );\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Add the new label with timeout.\n        match tokio::time::timeout(\n            std::time::Duration::from_secs(30),\n            store.add_label(&bead.id, &new_label),\n        )\n        .await\n        {\n            Ok(Ok(())) => {\n                tracing::debug!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    count = new_count,\n                    \"failure count incremented\"\n                );\n            }\n            Ok(Err(e)) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"failed to add failure-count label\"\n                );\n            }\n            Err(_) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    \"add_label timed out after 30s\"\n                );\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Outcome Display\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nimpl fmt::Display for Outcome {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            Outcome::Success => write!(f, \"Success\"),\n            Outcome::Failure => write!(f, \"Failure\"),\n            Outcome::Timeout => write!(f, \"Timeout\"),\n            Outcome::AgentNotFound => write!(f, \"AgentNotFound\"),\n            Outcome::Interrupted => write!(f, \"Interrupted\"),\n            Outcome::Crash(code) => write!(f, \"Crash({})\", code),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Tests\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::bead_store::Filters;\n    use crate::telemetry::Sink;\n    use crate::types::{BeadId, ClaimResult};\n    use async_trait::async_trait;\n    use chrono::Utc;\n    use std::path::PathBuf;\n    use std::sync::Mutex;\n\n    // ── Mock BeadStore ──\n\n    #[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n    #[allow(dead_code)] // Fields read via pattern matching in test assertions\n    enum StoreAction {\n        Release(String),\n        Reopen(String),\n        AddLabel(String, String),\n        RemoveLabel(String, String),\n        Show(String),\n        CreateBead(String, String),\n        AddDependency(String, String),\n    }\n\n    struct MockBeadStore {\n        actions: Mutex<Vec<StoreAction>>,\n        show_status: BeadStatus,\n        labels: Vec<String>,\n    }\n\n    impl MockBeadStore {\n        fn new(show_status: BeadStatus) -> Self {\n            MockBeadStore {\n                actions: Mutex::new(Vec::new()),\n                show_status,\n                labels: Vec::new(),\n            }\n        }\n\n        fn with_labels(mut self, labels: Vec<String>) -> Self {\n            self.labels = labels;\n            self\n        }\n\n        fn actions(&self) -> Vec<StoreAction> {\n            self.actions.lock().unwrap().clone()\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn test_bead(status: BeadStatus) -> Bead {\n        Bead {\n            id: BeadId::from(\"needle-test\"),\n            title: \"Test bead\".to_string(),\n            body: Some(\"Test body\".to_string()),\n            priority: 1,\n            status,\n            assignee: Some(\"worker-01\".to_string()),\n            labels: vec![],\n            workspace: PathBuf::from(\"/tmp\"),\n            dependencies: vec![],\n            dependents: vec![],\n            created_at: Utc::now(),\n            updated_at: Utc::now(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[async_trait]\n    impl BeadStore for MockBeadStore {\n        async fn list_all(&self) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n            Ok(vec![])\n        }\n        async fn ready(&self, _filters: &Filters) -> Result<Vec<Bead>> {\n            Ok(vec![])\n        }\n        async fn show(&self, id: &BeadId) -> Result<Bead> {\n            self.actions\n                .lock()\n                .unwrap()\n                .push(StoreAction::Show(id.to_string()));\n            Ok(test_bead(self.show_status.clone()))\n        }\n        async fn claim(&self, _id: &BeadId, _actor: &str) -> Result<ClaimResult> {\n            Ok(ClaimResult::NotClaimable {\n                reason: \"mock\".to_string(),\n            })\n        }\n        async fn release(&self, id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            self.actions\n                .lock()\n                .unwrap()\n                .push(StoreAction::Release(id.to_string()));\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn flush(&self) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn reopen(&self, id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            self.actions\n                .lock()\n                .unwrap()\n                .push(StoreAction::Reopen(id.to_string()));\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn labels(&self, _id: &BeadId) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n            Ok(self.labels.clone())\n        }\n        async fn add_label(&self, id: &BeadId, label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n            self.actions\n                .lock()\n                .unwrap()\n                .push(StoreAction::AddLabel(id.to_string(), label.to_string()));\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn remove_label(&self, id: &BeadId, label: &str) -> Result<()> {\n            self.actions\n                .lock()\n                .unwrap()\n                .push(StoreAction::RemoveLabel(id.to_string(), label.to_string()));\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn create_bead(&self, title: &str, body: &str, _labels: &[&str]) -> Result<BeadId> {\n            self.actions\n                .lock()\n                .unwrap()\n                .push(StoreAction::CreateBead(title.to_string(), body.to_string()));\n            Ok(BeadId::from(\"alert-001\"))\n        }\n        async fn doctor_repair(&self) -> Result<crate::bead_store::RepairReport> {\n            Ok(crate::bead_store::RepairReport::default())\n        }\n        async fn doctor_check(&self) -> Result<crate::bead_store::RepairReport> {\n            Ok(crate::bead_store::RepairReport::default())\n        }\n        async fn full_rebuild(&self) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn add_dependency(&self, blocker_id: &BeadId, blocked_id: &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n            self.actions\n                .lock()\n                .unwrap()\n                .push(StoreAction::AddDependency(\n                    blocker_id.to_string(),\n                    blocked_id.to_string(),\n                ));\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        async fn remove_dependency(\n            &self,\n            _blocked_id: &BeadId,\n            _blocker_id: &BeadId,\n        ) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n    }\n\n    struct NopSink;\n\n    impl Sink for NopSink {\n        fn accept(&self, _event: &crate::telemetry::TelemetryEvent) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        fn flush(&self, _deadline: std::time::Duration) -> Result<()> {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn test_handler() -> OutcomeHandler {\n        let config = Config::default();\n        let telemetry = Telemetry::with_sink(\"test-worker\".to_string(), NopSink);\n        OutcomeHandler::new(config, telemetry)\n    }\n\n    fn test_output(exit_code: i32) -> AgentOutcome {\n        AgentOutcome {\n            exit_code,\n            stdout: String::new(),\n            stderr: String::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ── classify tests ──\n\n    #[test]\n    fn classify_was_interrupted_always_returns_interrupted() {\n        assert_eq!(classify(0, true), Outcome::Interrupted);\n        assert_eq!(classify(1, true), Outcome::Interrupted);\n        assert_eq!(classify(127, true), Outcome::Interrupted);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn classify_not_interrupted_uses_exit_code() {\n        assert_eq!(classify(0, false), Outcome::Success);\n        assert_eq!(classify(1, false), Outcome::Failure);\n        assert_eq!(classify(124, false), Outcome::Timeout);\n        assert_eq!(classify(127, false), Outcome::AgentNotFound);\n        assert_eq!(classify(129, false), Outcome::Crash(129));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn classify_no_wildcard_arms() {\n        // Verify key exit codes map correctly per spec.\n        assert_eq!(classify(0, false), Outcome::Success);\n        assert_eq!(classify(1, false), Outcome::Failure);\n        assert_eq!(classify(2, false), Outcome::Failure);\n        assert_eq!(classify(99, false), Outcome::Failure);\n        assert_eq!(classify(100, false), Outcome::Failure);\n        assert_eq!(classify(124, false), Outcome::Timeout);\n        assert_eq!(classify(125, false), Outcome::Failure);\n        assert_eq!(classify(128, false), Outcome::Failure); // not >128 per spec\n        assert_eq!(classify(129, false), Outcome::Crash(129));\n        assert_eq!(classify(137, false), Outcome::Crash(137));\n        assert_eq!(classify(-9, false), Outcome::Crash(-9));\n    }\n\n    // ── handle tests ──\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn handle_success_bead_closed_by_agent() {\n        let handler = test_handler();\n        let store = MockBeadStore::new(BeadStatus::Done);\n        let bead = test_bead(BeadStatus::InProgress);\n\n        let result = handler\n            .handle(&store, &bead, &test_output(0), false)\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(result.outcome, Outcome::Success);\n        assert_eq!(result.bead_action, BeadAction::None);\n        assert!(!result.telemetry_events.is_empty());\n        let actions = store.actions();\n        assert!(\n            !actions.iter().any(|a| matches!(a, StoreAction::Release(_))),\n            \"success should not release bead\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn handle_success_bead_still_open_emits_orphaned() {\n        let handler = test_handler();\n        let store = MockBeadStore::new(BeadStatus::InProgress);\n        let bead = test_bead(BeadStatus::InProgress);\n\n        let result = handler\n            .handle(&store, &bead, &test_output(0), false)\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(result.outcome, Outcome::Success);\n        assert_eq!(result.bead_action, BeadAction::None);\n        let actions = store.actions();\n        assert!(\n            actions.iter().any(|a| matches!(a, StoreAction::Show(_))),\n            \"success should check bead status\"\n        );\n        assert!(result\n            .telemetry_events\n            .iter()\n            .any(|e| matches!(e, EventKind::BeadOrphaned { .. })));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn handle_failure_releases_and_increments_count() {\n        let handler = test_handler();\n        let store = MockBeadStore::new(BeadStatus::InProgress);\n        let bead = test_bead(BeadStatus::InProgress);\n\n        let result = handler\n            .handle(&store, &bead, &test_output(1), false)\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(result.outcome, Outcome::Failure);\n        assert_eq!(result.bead_action, BeadAction::Released);\n        assert!(!result.telemetry_events.is_empty());\n\n        let actions = store.actions();\n        assert!(\n            actions\n                .iter()\n                .any(|a| matches!(a, StoreAction::Release(id) if id == \"needle-test\")),\n            \"failure must release bead\"\n        );\n        assert!(\n            actions.iter().any(\n                |a| matches!(a, StoreAction::AddLabel(_, label) if label == \"failure-count:1\")\n            ),\n            \"failure must add failure-count:1\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn handle_failure_increments_existing_count() {\n        let handler = test_handler();\n        let store = MockBeadStore::new(BeadStatus::InProgress)\n            .with_labels(vec![\"failure-count:2\".to_string()]);\n        let bead = test_bead(BeadStatus::InProgress);\n\n        let result = handler\n            .handle(&store, &bead, &test_output(1), false)\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(result.bead_action, BeadAction::Released);\n        let actions = store.actions();\n        assert!(\n            actions.iter().any(\n                |a| matches!(a, StoreAction::RemoveLabel(_, label) if label == \"failure-count:2\")\n            ),\n            \"should remove old failure-count label\"\n        );\n        assert!(\n            actions.iter().any(\n                |a| matches!(a, StoreAction::AddLabel(_, label) if label == \"failure-count:3\")\n            ),\n            \"should add failure-count:3\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn handle_timeout_releases_and_adds_deferred() {\n        let handler = test_handler();\n        let store = MockBeadStore::new(BeadStatus::InProgress);\n        let bead = test_bead(BeadStatus::InProgress);\n\n        let result = handler\n            .handle(&store, &bead, &test_output(124), false)\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(result.outcome, Outcome::Timeout);\n        assert_eq!(result.bead_action, BeadAction::Deferred);\n\n        let actions = store.actions();\n        assert!(\n            actions\n                .iter()\n                .any(|a| matches!(a, StoreAction::Release(id) if id == \"needle-test\")),\n            \"timeout must release bead\"\n Both reached state HANDLING after an unsuccessful agent run, then went silent.\n\n**delta** (workspace: hoop-ttb) — last events:\n```\nagent.dispatched       bead_id=hoop-ttb.6.6  prompt_len=1079\ntransform.skipped      reason=not configured\nagent.completed        duration_ms=12355  exit_code=1\nEXECUTING -> HANDLING\noutcome.classified     outcome=failure  exit_code=1\n(silence for 9h)\n```\n\n**foxtrot** (workspace: hoop-ttb) — last events:\n```\nagent.dispatched       bead_id=hoop-ttb.7.3  prompt_len=921\ntransform.skipped      reason=not configured\nagent.completed        duration_ms=600002  exit_code=124\nEXECUTING -> HANDLING\noutcome.classified     outcome=timeout  exit_code=124\n(silence for 9h)\n```\n\n## Root cause hypothesis\n\nThe HANDLING state is supposed to release the bead (`br update --status open --assignee ''`) and transition to LOGGING or SELECTING, but there's a code path that blocks indefinitely — likely the `br` subprocess call with no timeout, or an unawaited future.\n\nSupporting evidence: foxtrot's stderr log contains an older `br release` sync-conflict error from 2026-04-22 22:45 — suggesting `br` calls can fail mid-HANDLING and the worker has no recovery.\n\n## Proposed fix\n\n1. Wrap all `br` subprocess calls in HANDLING with a timeout (30s, then retry with backoff, then fail the worker with a `worker.handling.timeout` event).\n2. On `br` failure during HANDLING, do not block — log the failure to telemetry and transition anyway; the bead can be reconciled later.\n3. Emit a heartbeat within HANDLING every 5s so we can detect future hangs externally.\n\n## Reproduction\n\nRun a worker against a failing bead; simulate a hung or slow `br update`.\n\n## Impact\n\n- delta, foxtrot wedged for 9+ hours each. No work done, no exit, no recovery.\n- Same failure mode affected lima (stuck EXECUTING/HANDLING for 25m) and may affect others under failure conditions.\n\n## Related\n- needle-fpu3 (dies silent after 900s idle) — different state (idle vs HANDLING).","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"fdc19704-1fdc-4a52-ab64-936db1c026b3","uuid":"f9159a36-6de0-4fd8-8813-efd28d681575","timestamp":"2026-04-26T11:06:37.053Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"◐ needle-qdgc · Worker hangs indefinitely in HANDLING state after agent failure or timeout   [● P0 · IN_PROGRESS]\nOwner: coding · Type: bug\nCreated: 2026-04-24 · Updated: 2026-04-26\nAssignee: charlie\nLabels: deferred, failure-count:188\n\n## Observed 2026-04-24\n\nWorkers **delta** and **foxtrot** stopped emitting any events for >9 hours while their parent processes remained alive. Both reached state HANDLING after an unsuccessful agent run, then went silent.\n\n**delta** (workspace: hoop-ttb) — last events:\n```\nagent.dispatched       bead_id=hoop-ttb.6.6  prompt_len=1079\ntransform.skipped      reason=not configured\nagent.completed        duration_ms=12355  exit_code=1\nEXECUTING -> HANDLING\noutcome.classified     outcome=failure  exit_code=1\n(silence for 9h)\n```\n\n**foxtrot** (workspace: hoop-ttb) — last events:\n```\nagent.dispatched       bead_id=hoop-ttb.7.3  prompt_len=921\ntransform.skipped      reason=not configured\nagent.completed        duration_ms=600002  exit_code=124\nEXECUTING -> HANDLING\noutcome.classified     outcome=timeout  exit_code=124\n(silence for 9h)\n```\n\n## Root cause hypothesis\n\nThe HANDLING state is supposed to release the bead (`br update --status open --assignee ''`) and transition to LOGGING or SELECTING, but there's a code path that blocks indefinitely — likely the `br` subprocess call with no timeout, or an unawaited future.\n\nSupporting evidence: foxtrot's stderr log contains an older `br release` sync-conflict error from 2026-04-22 22:45 — suggesting `br` calls can fail mid-HANDLING and the worker has no recovery.\n\n## Proposed fix\n\n1. Wrap all `br` subprocess calls in HANDLING with a timeout (30s, then retry with backoff, then fail the worker with a `worker.handling.timeout` event).\n2. On `br` failure during HANDLING, do not block — log the failure to telemetry and transition anyway; the bead can be reconciled later.\n3. Emit a heartbeat within HANDLING every 5s so we can detect future hangs externally.\n\n## Reproduction\n\nRun a worker against a failing bead; simulate a hung or slow `br update`.\n\n## Impact\n\n- delta, foxtrot wedged for 9+ hours each. This misses a real failure mode: **a worker is alive, emitting fresh heartbeats, but making no progress** \\u2014 spinning in a bounded state loop indefinitely.\\n\\n## Observed 2026-04-24\\n\\nWorkers **juliet** and **mike** (workspace: telegram-claude-bridge) ran for ~7 hours producing 238 MB and 236 MB of JSONL respectively (~4M / 1.1M lines). Every heartbeat was fresh \\u2014 Splice would have deemed them healthy. The log pattern was:\\n\\n```\\nSELECTING -> CLAIMING\\nbead.claim.attempted  bead_id=telegram-claude-bridge-mc3  attempt=1\\nbead.claim.race_lost  bead_id=telegram-claude-bridge-mc3\\nCLAIMING -> RETRYING\\nRETRYING -> SELECTING\\nSELECTING -> CLAIMING\\nbead.claim.attempted  bead_id=telegram-claude-bridge-mc3  attempt=1   (same bead)\\nbead.claim.race_lost  bead_id=telegram-claude-bridge-mc3\\n... (repeats ~1000x/sec)\\n```\\n\\nRoot-cause fix is tracked in **needle-aad8** (selector returns same bead id after failed claim). This bead is about **detection** \\u2014 making sure Splice catches the pattern even once the root cause recurs in a new form.\\n\\n## Proposed detection\\n\\nAdd to Splice (or a sibling \\\"Pulse\\\" check running at the same waterfall position) a tail-of-JSONL analysis per live worker:\\n\\n1. For each worker with a fresh heartbeat, read the last N events from its JSONL (e.g. N=200, bounded by mmap + tail scan \\u2014 do not ingest gigabyte files).\\n2. Pattern 1 (**claim churn**): >= X `bead.claim.race_lost` events in the last N with the same `bead_id` (X=20, configurable).\\n3. Pattern 2 (**state ping-pong**): >= X consecutive `worker.state_transition` events forming a short cycle (length <= 4) with no `agent.dispatched`, `bead.claim.succeeded`, or other forward-progress event interleaved.\\n4. Pattern 3 (**log-rate runaway**): JSONL file grew by > threshold MB in last M seconds without any `agent.completed` event (e.g. > 10 MB in 5 min).\\n\\nOn match: file a failure bead (same reporting path as Splice) with the matched pattern and a sample event window. De-duplicate by `(worker_id, session_id, pattern_name)` in `documented_sessions`.\\n\\n## Config additions to SpliceConfig\\n\\n```rust\\n/// Whether to scan live workers' JSONL for stuck-loop patterns (default: true).\\npub detect_live_loops: bool,\\n\\n/// Max events to scan from JSONL tail per worker (default: 200).\\npub live_loop_scan_events: usize,\\n\\n/// Min same-bead race_lost events to flag as claim churn (default: 20).\\npub claim_churn_threshold: u32,\\n\\n/// Max JSONL growth (bytes) in live_loop_window_secs without agent.completed (default: 10 MiB).\\npub log_runaway_bytes: u64,\\n\\n/// Window for log-rate check (default: 300s).\\npub live_loop_window_secs: u64,\\n```\\n\\n## Out of scope\\n\\n- **Fixing** the loops. That's needle-aad8 (race-lost), needle-qdgc (HANDLING hang), etc.\\n- **Restarting** looping workers. Splice's design choice is report-only; this bead keeps that property.\\n\\n## Related\\n- needle-w17l (SpliceStrand impl) \\u2014 extend its scan to include live workers\\n- needle-2yxu (SpliceConfig) \\u2014 add the knobs above\\n- needle-aad8 (root cause for juliet/mike) \\u2014 this bead is the detection side of the same problem\", \"status\": \"closed\", \"priority\": 1, \"issue_type\": \"bug\", \"assignee\": \"bravo\", \"created_at\": \"2026-04-24T16:38:59.500968412Z\", \"created_by\": \"coding\", \"updated_at\": \"2026-04-25T00:08:02.363140620Z\", \"closed_at\": \"2026-04-25T00:08:02.363013758Z\", \"close_reason\": \"Implemented live loop detection in SpliceStrand. Three patterns are now detected:\\n\\n1. **Claim churn** (Pattern 1): `detect_claim_churn()` checks for >= `claim_churn_threshold` (default: 20) `bead.claim.race_lost` events for the same bead in the last `live_loop_scan_events` (default: 200).\\n\\n2. **State ping-pong** (Pattern 2): `detect_state_ping_pong()` identifies short state cycles (<= 4 unique states) without forward-progress events like `agent.completed` or `bead.completed`.\\n\\n3. **Log runaway** (Pattern 3): `detect_log_runaway()` flags workers whose JSONL grew > `log_runaway_bytes` (default: 10 MiB) within `live_loop_window_secs` (default: 300) without any completion events.\\n\\nThe `scan_live_loops()` method iterates over workers with fresh heartbeats, reads the tail of each worker's JSONL file, and runs all three pattern detectors. When dead worker entries accumulate (see related bead: mend deregistration bug), this counter inflates and blocks all dispatches.\\n\\nHowever, even if mend properly deregisters dead workers, the rate limiter should be resilient to stale registry state. Currently the counter can only go up (on register) and relies on deregister to go down \\u2014 there is no periodic reconciliation.\\n\\n## Proposed Fix\\n\\nThe rate limiter's concurrency check should validate that counted workers are actually alive (PID exists) rather than trusting the registry count at face value. Options:\\n\\n1. **PID liveness check at dispatch time**: Before blocking on `concurrency exceeded`, verify each registered worker's PID is alive. Skip dead entries in the count.\\n2. **Periodic reconciliation**: Add a background sweep (every N seconds) that prunes dead entries from the concurrency counter, independent of mend's schedule.\\n3. **Heartbeat-based counting**: Use heartbeat freshness instead of registry presence for concurrency \\u2014 a worker without a recent heartbeat doesn't count.\\n\\nOption 1 is the simplest and most robust \\u2014 it makes the rate limiter self-healing regardless of whether mend runs.\\n\\n## Acceptance Criteria\\n\\n- [ ] Rate limiter concurrency check skips workers whose PID no longer exists\\n- [ ] A worker that dies mid-dispatch does not permanently consume a concurrency slot\\n- [ ] Test: register 10 workers with dead PIDs, verify new dispatch is not blocked\", \"status\": \"closed\", \"priority\": 1, \"issue_type\": \"bug\", \"assignee\": \"juliet\", \"created_at\": \"2026-04-14T00:28:58.456783607Z\", \"created_by\": \"coding\", \"updated_at\": \"2026-04-20T12:19:54.688077528Z\", \"closed_at\": \"2026-04-20T12:19:54.688014685Z\", \"close_reason\": \"done\", \"source_repo\": \".\", \"compaction_level\": 0, \"original_size\": 0, \"labels\": [\"failure-count:328\"]}\n{\"id\": \"needle-hz6\", \"title\": \"Agent dispatch with doc files as context\", \"description\": \"## Task\\nAgent dispatch with doc files as context\\n\\n## Context\\nThis task was decomposed from parent bead. Fix heartbeat task abort timing - abort in early return paths (error/timeout)\n      not just at the end of successful handling\n    - Add timeout protection to mitosis evaluation br calls (labels, list_all)\n    - Reduce idle heartbeat interval from 60s to 5s for better visibility\n    - Add heartbeat telemetry emission during idle sleep\n    \n    This ensures workers recover gracefully when `br` hangs or fails, rather\n    than wedging for hours with no telemetry output.\n    \n    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>\n\n .beads/issues.jsonl           |   7 +-\n .beads/learnings.md           |   8 +--\n .needle-predispatch-sha       |   2 +-\n src/health/mod.rs             | 160 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++-\n src/mitosis/mod.rs            |  57 ++++++++++++---\n src/strand/mend.rs            |  36 ++++++----\n src/worker/mod.rs             |  24 +++++--\n tests/p2_integration_tests.rs |   1 +\n 8 files changed, 256 insertions(+), 39 deletions(-)","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"fdc19704-1fdc-4a52-ab64-936db1c026b3","uuid":"5bfc04dc-3947-435e-97ea-935053fd4a7b","timestamp":"2026-04-26T11:06:42.372Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"commit eaf0cc7ec714117e160b869854674de9d2cb371f\nAuthor: jedarden <github@jedarden.com>\nDate:   Fri Apr 24 14:17:30 2026 -0400\n\n    fix(needle-qdgc): prevent worker hang in HANDLING state after agent failure\n    \n    Workers were getting stuck indefinitely in HANDLING state after agent\n    failure or timeout because `br` subprocess calls (release, reopen, show,\n    add_label, remove_label, create_bead) had no timeout protection.\n    \n    Changes:\n    - Fix heartbeat task abort timing - abort in early return paths (error/timeout)\n      not just at the end of successful handling\n    - Add timeout protection to mitosis evaluation br calls (labels, list_all)\n    - Reduce idle heartbeat interval from 60s to 5s for better visibility\n    - Add heartbeat telemetry emission during idle sleep\n    \n    This ensures workers recover gracefully when `br` hangs or fails, rather\n    than wedging for hours with no telemetry output.\n    \n    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>\n\n .beads/issues.jsonl           |   7 +-\n .beads/learnings.md           |   8 +--\n .needle-predispatch-sha       |   2 +-\n src/health/mod.rs             | 160 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++-\n src/mitosis/mod.rs            |  57 ++++++++++++---\n src/strand/mend.rs            |  36 ++++++----\n src/worker/mod.rs             |  24 +++++--\n tests/p2_integration_tests.rs |   1 +\n 8 files changed, 256 insertions(+), 39 deletions(-)","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_ef278018bd404e2987ae4a97","type":"tool_result","content":"commit 30acb6797eff9fd22ebbf9ffa5ca7dcd94b902ec\nAuthor: jedarden <github@jedarden.com>\nDate:   Sat Apr 25 20:45:01 2026 -0400\n\n    fix(needle-qdgc): prevent worker hang in HANDLING state when telemetry is wedged\n    \n    The root cause of workers delta and foxtrot hanging for 9+ hours in HANDLING\n    state was a blocking telemetry.emit() call in set_state(). When the telemetry\n    mutex is contended (writer thread stuck), emit() blocks indefinitely on\n    std::sync::Mutex::lock(), preventing state transitions from completing.\n    \n    Workers enter HANDLING after agent completion and must transition to LOGGING\n    or SELECTING to continue processing. The set_state() function was called with\n    a blocking emit() at each transition point. If telemetry was wedged, the\n    worker would hang forever — no state transition, no further heartbeats, no\n    recovery.\n    \n    Fix: Change set_state() to use emit_try_lock() instead of emit(). This allows\n    state transitions to complete even when telemetry is wedged. The heartbeat\n    shared state (health.update_state) is always updated, so monitoring can detect\n    the new state via heartbeat files even if the telemetry event was skipped.\n    \n    This change makes state transitions non-blocking in all paths, including:\n    - HANDLING -> LOGGING (after handler completion/error/timeout)\n    - HANDLING -> Stopped (after interrupted outcome)\n    - All other state transitions throughout the worker lifecycle\n    \n    Related: needle-fpu3 (dies silent after 900s idle) may have a similar root\n    cause if the idle heartbeat path uses blocking emit().\n    \n    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>\n\n .../beads.db-journal.20260425_212637_112806702.bak |  Bin 0 -> 512 bytes\n Structured telemetry — JSONL event stream, never on stdout/stderr.\n//!\n//! Every state transition, claim attempt, dispatch, and outcome emits a typed\n//! event. The emitter is non-blocking: events are queued and written by a\n//! background task. A broken sink never blocks or panics the worker.\n//!\n//! ## Architecture\n//! ```text\n//! worker → emit() → mpsc::Sender → [background task] → TelemetryBus → Vec<Box<dyn Sink>>\n//! ```\n//!\n//! Depends on: `types`.\n\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\nuse std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU64, Ordering};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\n\nuse anyhow::{Context, Result};\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse tokio::sync::mpsc;\n\nuse crate::config::{ColorMode, HookConfig, StdoutFormat, StdoutSinkConfig, TelemetryConfig};\nuse crate::types::{BeadId, WorkerId, WorkerState};\n\n// ─── OTLP Sink (feature-gated) ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[cfg(feature = \"otlp\")]\npub mod otlp;\n\n#[cfg(feature = \"otlp\")]\npub use otlp::OtlpSink;\n\n// ─── Test Utilities ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[cfg(test)]\npub mod test_utils;\n\n// ─── TelemetryEvent ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// A single structured telemetry record.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TelemetryEvent {\n    /// UTC timestamp with millisecond precision.\n    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,\n    /// Discriminant string, e.g. `\"state_transition\"`.\n    pub event_type: String,\n    /// The worker that emitted this event.\n    pub worker_id: WorkerId,\n    /// Session identifier (8 hex chars, unique per worker boot).\n    pub session_id: String,\n    /// Monotonically increasing counter within the session.\n    pub sequence: u64,\n    /// Bead context when relevant.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub bead_id: Option<BeadId>,\n    /// Workspace directory when relevant.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub workspace: Option<PathBuf>,\n    /// Event-specific payload as a JSON value.\n    pub data: serde_json::Value,\n    /// Optional duration in milliseconds for timed operations.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub duration_ms: Option<u64>,\n    /// W3C trace-id hex (32 lowercase hex chars). Present only when emitted inside an OTel span.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub trace_id: Option<String>,\n    /// W3C span-id hex (16 lowercase hex chars). Present only when emitted inside an OTel span.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub span_id: Option<String>,\n}\n\n// ─── EventKind ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Typed event variants emitted by all NEEDLE components.\n///\n/// Every variant maps to a `TelemetryEvent` with `event_type` matching\n/// the snake_case discriminant name.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n#[non_exhaustive]\npub enum EventKind {\n    // ── Worker lifecycle ──\n    WorkerBooting {\n        worker_name: String,\n        version: String,\n    },\n    WorkerStarted {\n        worker_name: String,\n        version: String,\n    },\n    WorkerStopped {\n        reason: String,\n        beads_processed: u64,\n        uptime_secs: u64,\n    },\n    WorkerErrored {\n        error_type: String,\n        error_message: String,\n        beads_processed: u64,\n    },\n    WorkerExhausted {\n        cycle_count: u64,\n        last_strand: String,\n        /// How many times the waterfall restarted before exhausting.\n        waterfall_restarts: u32,\n        /// Name of each strand that returned WorkCreated (one entry per restart).\n        restart_triggers: Vec<String>,\n        /// All strand evaluations in order, across all waterfall passes.\n        /// Each entry is (strand_name, result, duration_ms) for diagnostic visibility.\n        strand_evaluations: Vec<(String, String, u64)>,\n    },\n    WorkerIdle {\n        backoff_seconds: u64,\n    },\n    IdleSleepCompleted {\n        backoff_secs: u64,\n        elapsed_secs: u64,\n        shutdown_checks: u64,\n    },\n    IdleSleepEntered {\n        backoff_secs: u64,\n        beads_processed: u64,\n        uptime_secs: u64,\n    },\n    StateTransition {\n        from: WorkerState,\n        to: WorkerState,\n    },\n    InitStepStarted {\n        step: String,\n    },\n    InitStepCompleted {\n        step: String,\n        duration_ms: u64,\n    },\n    WorkerBootTimeout {\n        elapsed_ms: u64,\n    },\n\n    // ── Strand evaluation ──\n    StrandEvaluated {\n        strand_name: String,\n        result: String,\n        duration_ms: u64,\n    },\n    StrandSkipped {\n        strand_name: String,\n        reason: String,\n    },\n    QueueEmpty,\n\n    // ── Bead claim ──\n    ClaimAttempt {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        attempt: u32,\n    },\n    ClaimSuccess {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        priority: i32,\n        strand: String,\n    },\n    ClaimRaceLost {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n    },\n    ClaimFailed {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        reason: String,\n    },\n    ClaimRaceLostSkipped {\n        consecutive_losses: u32,\n        threshold: u32,\n    },\n\n    // ── Bead lifecycle ──\n    BeadReleased {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        reason: String,\n    },\n    BeadReleaseFailed {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        reason: String,\n    },\n    BeadCompleted {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        duration_ms: u64,\n    },\n    BeadOrphaned {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n    },\n\n    // ── Agent dispatch ──\n    DispatchStarted {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        agent: String,\n        prompt_len: usize,\n        /// Template name used to build the prompt (e.g., `\"pluck\"`).\n        template_name: String,\n        /// Version tag identifying which variant was used (e.g., `\"pluck-default\"`, `\"pluck-v2\"`).\n        template_version: String,\n        /// SHA-256 hex digest of the rendered prompt content.\n        prompt_hash: String,\n    },\n    DispatchCompleted {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        exit_code: i32,\n        duration_ms: u64,\n        agent: String,\n        model: Option<String>,\n    },\n    BuildTimeout {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        timeout_secs: u64,\n    },\n    BuildHeartbeat {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        elapsed_ms: u64,\n    },\n\n    // ── Outcome ──\n    OutcomeClassified {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        outcome: String,\n        exit_code: i32,\n    },\n    OutcomeHandled {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        outcome: String,\n        action: String,\n    },\n    WorkerHandlingTimeout {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        outcome: String,\n        operation: String,\n        error: String,\n    },\n\n    // ── Health ──\n    HeartbeatEmitted {\n        bead_id: Option<BeadId>,\n        state: String,\n    },\n    StuckDetected {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        age_secs: u64,\n    },\n    StuckReleased {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        peer_worker: String,\n    },\n    HealthCheck {\n        db_healthy: bool,\n        disk_free_mb: u64,\n        peer_count: u32,\n    },\n\n    // ── Mend strand ──\n    MendOrphanedLockRemoved {\n        lock_path: String,\n        age_secs: u64,\n    },\n    MendDependencyCleaned {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        blocker_id: BeadId,\n    },\n    MendDbRepaired {\n        warnings: u32,\n        fixed: u32,\n    },\n    MendDbRebuilt,\n    MendCycleSummary {\n        beads_released: u32,\n        locks_removed: u32,\n        deps_cleaned: u32,\n        db_repaired: bool,\n        db_rebuilt: bool,\n        agent_logs_cleaned: u32,\n        zero_activity_logs_cleaned: u32,\n        traces_pruned: u32,\n        traces_deleted: u32,\n        workers_deregistered: u32,\n        idle_workers_flagged: u32,\n        rate_limits_cleaned: u32,\n    },\n    MendTraceCleanup {\n        traces_pruned: u32,\n        traces_deleted: u32,\n    },\n    MendLearningCleanup {\n        pruned: u32,\n        consolidated: u32,\n    },\n    MendIdleWorkerFlagged {\n        worker_id: String,\n        pid: u32,\n        age_secs: u64,\n    },\n    MendWorkerDeregistered {\n        worker_id: String,\n        pid: u32,\n    },\n    MendOrphanedHeartbeatRemoved {\n        worker_id: String,\n        age_secs: u64,\n    },\n    MendDependencyRemoved {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        blocker_id: BeadId,\n    },\n    MendBeadReleaseFailed {\n        bead_id: String,\n        assignee: String,\n        error: String,\n    },\n    MendDependencyCleanupFailed {\n        bead_id: String,\n        blocker_id: String,\n        error: String,\n    },\n    MendLockRemoveFailed {\n        lock_path: String,\n        error: String,\n    },\n    MendRateLimitCleaned {\n        provider: String,\n        age_secs: u64,\n    },\n    MendRateLimitProviderRemoved {\n        provider: String,\n    },\n    MendRateLimitProviderReset {\n        provider: String,\n        age_secs: u64,\n    },\n    MendZeroActivityLogCleaned {\n        worker_id: String,\n        log_path: String,\n    },\n\n    // ── Effort tracking ──\n    EffortRecorded {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        elapsed_ms: u64,\n        agent_name: String,\n        model: Option<String>,\n        tokens_in: Option<u64>,\n        tokens_out: Option<u64>,\n        estimated_cost_usd: Option<f64>,\n    },\n    BudgetWarning {\n        daily_cost: f64,\n        threshold: f64,\n    },\n    BudgetStop {\n        daily_cost: f64,\n        threshold: f64,\n    },\n\n    // ── Rate limiting ──\n    RateLimitWait {\n        provider: String,\n        model: Option<String>,\n        reason: String,\n    },\n    RateLimitAllowed {\n        provider: String,\n        model: Option<String>,\n    },\n\n    // ── Mitosis ──\n    MitosisEvaluated {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        splittable: bool,\n        proposed_children: u32,\n    },\n    MitosisSplit {\n        parent_id: BeadId,\n        children_created: u32,\n        children_skipped: u32,\n        child_ids: Vec<BeadId>,\n    },\n    MitosisSkipped {\n        parent_id: BeadId,\n        existing_children: u32,\n    },\n\n    // ── Validation gates ──\n    VerificationFailed {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        command: String,\n        exit_code: Option<i32>,\n        output: String,\n    },\n    VerificationPassed {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        gates_run: u32,\n    },\n\n    // ── Unravel ──\n    UnravelAnalyzed {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        alternatives_proposed: u32,\n    },\n    UnravelSkipped {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        reason: String,\n    },\n\n    // ── Reflect ──\n    ReflectStarted {\n        beads_since_last: usize,\n    },\n    ReflectConsolidated {\n        learnings_added: usize,\n        learnings_pruned: usize,\n        skills_promoted: usize,\n        beads_processed: usize,\n    },\n    ReflectSkipped {\n        reason: String,\n    },\n\n    // ── Pulse ──\n    PulseScannerStarted {\n        scanner_name: String,\n    },\n    PulseScannerCompleted {\n        scanner_name: String,\n        findings_count: u32,\n    },\n    PulseScannerFailed {\n        scanner_name: String,\n        error: String,\n    },\n    PulseBeadCreated {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        scanner_name: String,\n        severity: u8,\n    },\n    PulseSkipped {\n        reason: String,\n    },\n\n    // ── Hot-reload ──\n    UpgradeDetected {\n        old_hash: String,\n        new_hash: String,\n    },\n    UpgradeCompleted {\n        new_hash: String,\n    },\n    RollbackCompleted {\n        rolled_back_hash: String,\n        restored_hash: String,\n    },\n\n    // ── Canary testing ──\n    CanaryStarted {\n        suite: String,\n    },\n    CanarySuiteCompleted {\n        suite: String,\n        passed: u32,\n        failed: u32,\n    },\n    CanaryPromoted {\n        hash: String,\n    },\n    CanaryRejected {\n        reason: String,\n    },\n\n    // ── Output transform ──\n    OutputTransformSpawned {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        transform_cmd: String,\n        log_path: String,\n    },\n    OutputTransformExited {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        exit_code: i32,\n    },\n    OutputTransformSkipped {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        reason: String,\n    },\n\n    // ── Transform lifecycle ──\n    TransformStarted {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        transform_binary: String,\n        agent: String,\n    },\n    TransformCompleted {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        events_written: u64,\n        duration_ms: u64,\n    },\n    TransformFailed {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        error: String,\n        exit_code: i32,\n    },\n    TransformSkipped {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        reason: String,\n    },\n\n    // ── Internal ──\n    SinkError {\n        message: String,\n    },\n    OtlpDropped {\n        signal: String,\n        dropped_count: u64,\n        queue_full: bool,\n    },\n    OtlpShutdownTimeout {\n        flushed_batches: u64,\n        remaining_batches: u64,\n    },\n}\n\nimpl EventKind {\n    /// Return the dotted event type string.\n    pub fn event_type(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            EventKind::WorkerBooting { .. } => \"worker.booting\",\n            EventKind::WorkerStarted { .. } => \"worker.started\",\n            EventKind::WorkerStopped { .. } => \"worker.stopped\",\n            EventKind::WorkerErrored { .. } => \"worker.errored\",\n            EventKind::WorkerExhausted { .. } => \"worker.exhausted\",\n            EventKind::WorkerIdle { .. } => \"worker.idle\",\n            EventKind::IdleSleepCompleted { .. } => \"worker.idle_sleep_completed\",\n            EventKind::IdleSleepEntered { .. } => \"worker.idle_sleep_entered\",\n \"init.step.completed\",\n            EventKind::WorkerBootTimeout { .. } => \"worker.boot.timeout\",\n            EventKind::StrandEvaluated { .. } => \"strand.evaluated\",\n            EventKind::StrandSkipped { .. } => \"strand.skipped\",\n            EventKind::QueueEmpty => \"worker.queue_empty\",\n            EventKind::ClaimAttempt { .. } => \"bead.claim.attempted\",\n            EventKind::ClaimSuccess { .. } => \"bead.claim.succeeded\",\n            EventKind::ClaimRaceLost { .. } => \"bead.claim.race_lost\",\n            EventKind::ClaimRaceLostSkipped { .. } => \"bead.claim.race_lost_skipped\",\n            EventKind::ClaimFailed { .. } => \"bead.claim.failed\",\n            EventKind::BeadReleased { .. } => \"bead.released\",\n            EventKind::BeadReleaseFailed { .. } => \"bead.release.failed\",\n            EventKind::BeadCompleted { .. } => \"bead.completed\",\n            EventKind::BeadOrphaned { .. } => \"bead.orphaned\",\n            EventKind::DispatchStarted { .. } => \"agent.dispatched\",\n            EventKind::DispatchCompleted { .. } => \"agent.completed\",\n            EventKind::BuildTimeout { .. } => \"build.timeout\",\n            EventKind::BuildHeartbeat { .. } => \"build.heartbeat\",\n            EventKind::OutcomeClassified { .. } => \"outcome.classified\",\n            EventKind::OutcomeHandled { .. } => \"outcome.handled\",\n            EventKind::WorkerHandlingTimeout { .. } => \"worker.handling.timeout\",\n            EventKind::HeartbeatEmitted { .. } => \"heartbeat.emitted\",\n            EventKind::StuckDetected { .. } => \"peer.stale\",\n            EventKind::StuckReleased { .. } => \"peer.crashed\",\n            EventKind::HealthCheck { .. } => \"health.check\",\n            EventKind::MendOrphanedLockRemoved { .. } => \"mend.orphaned_lock_removed\",\n            EventKind::MendDependencyCleaned { .. } => \"mend.dependency_cleaned\",\n            EventKind::MendDbRepaired { .. } => \"mend.db_repaired\",\n            EventKind::MendDbRebuilt => \"mend.db_rebuilt\",\n            EventKind::MendCycleSummary { .. } => \"mend.cycle_summary\",\n            EventKind::MendTraceCleanup { .. } => \"mend.trace_cleanup\",\n            EventKind::MendLearningCleanup { .. } => \"mend.learning_cleanup\",\n            EventKind::MendIdleWorkerFlagged { .. } => \"mend.idle_worker_flagged\",\n            EventKind::MendWorkerDeregistered { .. } => \"mend.worker_deregistered\",\n            EventKind::MendOrphanedHeartbeatRemoved { .. } => \"mend.orphaned_heartbeat_removed\",\n            EventKind::MendDependencyRemoved { .. } => \"mend.dependency_removed\",\n            EventKind::MendBeadReleaseFailed { .. } => \"mend.bead_release_failed\",\n            EventKind::MendDependencyCleanupFailed { .. } => \"mend.dependency_cleanup_failed\",\n            EventKind::MendLockRemoveFailed { .. } => \"mend.lock_remove_failed\",\n            EventKind::MendRateLimitCleaned { .. } => \"mend.rate_limit_cleaned\",\n            EventKind::MendRateLimitProviderRemoved { .. } => \"mend.rate_limit_provider_removed\",\n            EventKind::MendRateLimitProviderReset { .. } => \"mend.rate_limit_provider_reset\",\n
